### Active Power `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ActivePower`

#### Added
- Individual: [Active Power](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ActivePower)

- [Active Power](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ActivePower) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "
  An $Active Power$ is, under periodic conditions, the mean value, taken over one period $T$, of the instantaneous power $p$.
  In complex notation, $P = \mathbf{Re} \; \underline{S}$, where $\underline{S}$ is $\textit{complex power}$.
  "^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

### British Thermal Unit (International Definition) `http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/BTU_IT`

#### Added
- Individual: [British Thermal Unit (International Definition)](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/BTU_IT)

- [British Thermal Unit (International Definition)](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/BTU_IT) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "$\textit{British Thermal Unit}$ (BTU or Btu) is a traditional unit of energy equal to about $1.0550558526 \textit{ kilojoule}$.
  It is approximately the amount of energy needed to heat 1 pound (0.454 kg) of water from $39^{\circ}{F}$ to $40^{\circ}{F}$ .
  The unit is most often used in the power, steam generation, heating and air conditioning industries.
  In scientific contexts the BTU has largely been replaced by the SI unit of energy, the $joule$, though it may be used as a measure of agricultural energy production (BTU/kg).
  It is still used unofficially in metric English-speaking countries (such as Canada), and remains the standard unit of classification for air conditioning units manufactured and sold in many non-English-speaking metric countries.
  "^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [British Thermal Unit (International Definition)](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/BTU_IT) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [IMPERIAL](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/IMPERIAL)

- [British Thermal Unit (International Definition)](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/BTU_IT) [symbol](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/symbol) "Btu{IT}"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CO2Equivalent `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/CO2Equivalent`

#### Added
- Individual: [CO2Equivalent](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/CO2Equivalent)

- [CO2Equivalent](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/CO2Equivalent) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "The CO2 equivalent is a measure used to compare the emissions from various greenhouse gases
  on the basis of their global-warming potential (GWP), by converting amounts of other gases to the equivalent amount
  of carbon dioxide with the same global warming potential."^^[HTML](http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#HTML)

- [CO2Equivalent](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/CO2Equivalent) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E0L0I0M1H0T0D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E0L0I0M1H0T0D0)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): CO2 transport and storage `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010058`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): CO2 transport and storage](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010058)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): CO2 transport and storage](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010058) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'CO2 transport and storage' is a sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Carbon dioxide (CO2) capture and storage (CCS) involves the capture of CO2 from anthropogenic sources, its transport to a storage location and its long-term isolation from the atmosphere. Emissions associated with CO2 transport, injection and storage are covered under category 1C. Emissions (and reductions) associated with CO2 capture should be reported under the IPCC Sector in which capture takes place (e.g. Fuel Combustion or Industrial Activities)."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): CO2 transport and storage](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010058) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): N2O from product use `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010376`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): N2O from product use](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010376)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): N2O from product use](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010376) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'N2O from product use' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Evaporative emissions of nitrous oxide (N2O) can arise from various types of product use, including:
- Medical applications (anaesthetic use, analgesic use and veterinary use);
- Use as a propellant in aerosol products, primarily in food industry (pressure-packaged whipped cream, etc);
- Oxidising agent and etchant used in semiconductor manufacturing;
- Oxidising agent used, with acetylene, in atomic absorption spectrometry;
- Production of sodium azide, which is used to inflate airbags;
- Fuel oxidant in auto racing; and
- Oxidising agent in blowtorches used by jewelers and others."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): N2O from product use](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010376) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 3, Chapter 8.4.1, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): N2O from product use](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010376) [OEO_00010037](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010037) "CRF 2.G.3"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): SF6 and PFCs from other product use `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010375`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): SF6 and PFCs from other product use](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010375)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): SF6 and PFCs from other product use](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010375) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'SF6 and PFCs from other product use' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Identified remaining applications in this source category include:
- SF6 and PFCs used in military applications, particularly SF6 used in airborne radar systems, e.g., AWACS (Airborne Warning and Control System), and PFCs used as heat transfer fluids in high-powered electronic applications;
- SF6 used in equipment in university and research particle accelerators;
- SF6 used in equipment in industrial and medical particle accelerators;
- &lsquo;Adiabatic&rsquo; applications utilising the low permeability through rubber of SF6 and some PFCs, e.g., car tires and sport shoe soles;
- SF6 used in sound-proof windows;
- PFCs used as heat transfer fluids in commercial and consumer applications;
- PFCs used in cosmetics and in medical applications;
- Other uses e.g. gas-air tracer in research and leak detectors."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): SF6 and PFCs from other product use](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010375) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 3, Chapter 8.3, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): SF6 and PFCs from other product use](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010375) [OEO_00010037](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010037) "CRF 2.G.2"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): TFT flat panel display `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010364`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): TFT flat panel display](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010364)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): TFT flat panel display](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010364) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'TFT flat panel display' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Uses and emissions of predominantly CF4, CHF3, NF3 and SF6 during the fabrication of thin-film transistors (TFTs) on glass substrates for flat panel display manufacture. In addition to these gases, C2F6, C3F8 and c-C4F8 may also be used and emitted during the manufacture of thin and smart displays."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): TFT flat panel display](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010364) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): adipic acid production `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010346`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): adipic acid production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010346)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): adipic acid production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010346) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'adipic acid production is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Adipic acid is used in the manufacture of a large number of products including synthetic fibres, coatings, plastics, urethane foams, elastomers and synthetic lubricants. The production of Nylon 6.6 accounts for the bulk of adipic acid
use. The main greenhouse gas emitted from adipic acid production is nitrous oxide."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): aerosols](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010371)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): aerosols](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010371) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'aerosols' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Most aerosol packages now contain hydrocarbon (HC) as propellants but, in a small fraction of the total, HFCs and PFCs may be used as propellants or solvents. Emissions from aerosols usually occur shortly after production, on average six months after sale. During the use of aerosols, 100% of the chemical is emitted. The 5 main sources are metered dose inhalers (MDIs), personal care products (e.g. hair care, deodorant, shaving cream), household products (e.g. air-fresheners, oven and fabric cleaners), industrial products (e.g. special cleaning sprays such as those for operating electrical contact, lubricants, pipe-freezers) and other general products (e.g. silly string, tire inflators, claxons), although in some regions the use of such general products is restricted. The HFCs currently used as propellants are HFC 134a, HFC 227ea, and HFC 152a. The substance HFC 43 10mee and a PFC, perfluorohexane, are used as solvents in industrial aerosol products."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): aerosols](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010371) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): aluminium production `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010355`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): aluminium production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010355)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): aluminium production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010355) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'aluminium production' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Aluminium Production covers primary production of aluminium, except the emissions related to the use of fuel. Carbon dioxide emissions result from the electrochemical reduction reaction of alumina with a carbon-based anode.
Tetrafluoromethane (CF4) and hexafluoroethane (C2F6) are also produced intermittently. No greenhouse gases are produced in recycling of aluminium other than from the fuels uses for metal remelting. Sulphur hexafluoride (SF6)
emissions are not associated with primary aluminium production; however, casting of some high magnesium containing alloys does result in SF6 emissions and these emissions are accounted for in Section 2C4, Magnesium
Production."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): aluminium production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010355) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): ammonia production `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010344`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): ammonia production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010344)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): ammonia production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010344) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'ammonia' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Ammonia (NH3) is a major industrial chemical and the most important nitrogenous material produced. Ammonia gas is used directly as a fertilizer, in heat treating, paper pulping, nitric acid and nitrates manufacture, nitric acid ester and nitro compound manufacture, explosives of various types, and as a refrigerant. Amines, amides, and miscellaneous other organic compounds, such as urea, are made from ammonia. The main greenhouse gas emitted from NH3 production is CO2. CO2 used in the production of urea, a downstream process, should be subtracted from the CO2 generated and accounted for in the AFOLU Sector."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): ammonia production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010344) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

Solid waste composting and other biological treatment. Emissions from biogas facilities (anaerobic digestion) with energy production are reported in the Energy Sector (1A4)."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): caprolactam, gloxal and glyoxylic acid production `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010347`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): caprolactam, gloxal and glyoxylic acid production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010347)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): caprolactam, gloxal and glyoxylic acid production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010347) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'caprolactam, glyoxal and glyoxylic acid production' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Most of the annual production of caprolactam (NH(CH2)5CO) is consumed as the monomer for nylon-6 fibres and plastics, with a substantial proportion of the fibre used in carpet manufacturing. All commercial processes for the manufacture of caprolactam are based on either toluene or benzene. This subcategory also covers production of glyoxal (ethanedial) and glyoxylic acid production. The main greenhouse gas emitted from this subcategory is nitrous oxide."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): caprolactam, gloxal and glyoxylic acid production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010347) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): carbide production `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010348`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): carbide production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010348)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): carbide production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010348) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'carbide production' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
The production of carbide can result in emissions of CO2, CH4, CO and SO2. Silicon carbide is a significant artificial abrasive. It is produced from silica sand or quartz and petroleum coke. Calcium carbide is used in the production of acetylene, in the manufacture of cyanamide (a minor historical use), and as a reductant in electric arc steel furnaces. It is made from calcium carbonate (limestone) and carbon-containing reductant (petroleum coke)."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): domestic aviation `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010059`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): domestic aviation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010059)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): domestic aviation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010059) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'domestic aviation' is a transport sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Emissions from civil domestic passenger and freight traffic that departs and arrives in the same country (commercial, private, agriculture, etc.), including take-offs and landings for these flight stages. Note that this may include journeys of considerable length between two airports in a country (e.g. San Francisco to Honolulu). Exclude military, which should be reported under 1 A 5 b."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): domestic aviation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010059) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

Emissions from fuels used by vessels of all flags that depart and arrive in the same country (exclude fishing, which should be reported under 1 A 4 c iii, and military, which should be reported under 1 A 5 b). ### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): electrical equipment `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010374`

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): electrical equipment](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010374) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'electrical equipment' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Electrical equipment is used in the transmission and distribution of electricity above 1 kV. SF6 is used in gasinsulated switchgear (GIS), gas circuit breakers (GCB), gas-insulated transformers (GIT), gas-insulated lines (GIL), outdoor gas-insulated instrument transformers, reclosers, switches, ring main units and other equipment."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): electronics industry `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010170`

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): electronics industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010170) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'electronics industry' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Several advanced electronics manufacturing processes utilise fluorinated compounds (FCs) for plasma etching intricate patterns, cleaning reactor chambers, and temperature control. The specific electronic industry sectors include semiconductor, thin-film-transistor flat panel display (TFT-FPD), and photovoltaic (PV) manufacturing (collectively termed &lsquo;electronics industry&rsquo;)."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): enteric fermentation `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010179`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): enteric fermentation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010179)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): enteric fermentation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010179) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'enteric fermentation' is an agriculture, forestry and land use sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Methane emissions from herbivores as a by-product of enteric fermentation (a digestive process by which carbohydrates are broken down by micro-organisms into simple molecules for absorption into the bloodstream). Ruminant animals (e.g., cattle, sheep) are major sources with moderate amounts produced from non-ruminant animals (e.g., pigs, horses)."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): enteric fermentation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010179) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): ferroalloys production `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010354`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): ferroalloys production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010354)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): ferroalloys production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010354) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'ferroalloys' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Ferroalloys production covers emissions from primary metallurgical reduction production of the most common ferroalloys, i.e. ferro-silicon, silicon metal, ferro-manganese, silicon manganese, and ferro-chromium, excluding those
emissions relating to fuel use. From the production of these alloys, carbon dioxide (CO2), nitrous oxide (N2O), and methane (CH4) originating from ore- and reductant raw materials, is emitted."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): ferroalloys production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010354) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): fire protection `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010370`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): fire protection](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010370)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): fire protection](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010370) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'fire protection' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
There are two general types of fire protection (fire suppression) equipment that use greenhouse gases as partial replacements for halons: portable (streaming) equipment, and fixed (flooding) equipment. The non-ozone depleting, industrial gases HFCs, PFCs and more recently a fluoroketone are mainly used as substitutes for halons, typically halon 1301, in flooding equipment. PFCs played an early role in halon 1301 replacement but current use is limited to replenishment of previously installed systems. HFCs in portable equipment, typically replacing halon 1211, are available but have achieved very limited market acceptance due primarily to their high cost. PFC use in new portable extinguishers is currently limited to a small amount (few percent) in an HCFC blend."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): fire protection](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010370) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): fire protection](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010370) [OEO_00010037](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010037) "CRF 2.F.3"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): fire protection](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010370) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1401

- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): foam blowing agents](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010369)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): foam blowing agents](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010369) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'foam blowing agents' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
HFCs are being used as replacements for CFCs and HCFCs in foams, particularly in closed-cell insulation applications. Compounds that are being used include HFC-245fa, HFC-365mfc, HFC-227ea, HFC-134a, and HFC-152a. The processes and applications for which these various HFCs are being used include insulation boards and panels, pipe sections, sprayed systems and onecomponent gap filling foams. For open-cell foams, such as integral skin products for automotive steering wheels and facias, emissions of HFCs used as blowing agents are likely to occur during the manufacturing process. In closedcell foam, emissions not only occur during the manufacturing phase, but usually extend into the in-use phase and often the majority of emission occurs at the endof-life (de-commissioning losses). Accordingly, emissions can occur over a period of up to 50 years or even longer."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): foam blowing agents](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010369) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): foam blowing agents](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010369) [OEO_00010037](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010037) "CRF 2.F.2"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): foam blowing agents](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010369) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1401
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1440

move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): foam blowing agents](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010369) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "CRF sector (IPCC 2006): foam blowing agents"@en

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): foam blowing agents](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010369) Type [industry sector](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000227)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): foam blowing agents](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010369) [is defined by](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000504) [CRF sectors (IPCC 2006)](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000242)


### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): forest land `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010189`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): forest land](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010189)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): forest land](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010189) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'forest land' is an agriculture, forestry and land use sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Emissions and removals from lands with woody vegetation consistent with thresholds used to define forest land in the national GHG inventory, sub-divided into managed and unmanaged, and possibly also by climatic region, soil type and vegetation type as appropriate. It also includes systems with vegetation that currently fall below, but are expected to later exceed, the threshold values used by a country to define the forest land category."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): forest land](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010189) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): forest land](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010189) [OEO_00010037](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010037) "CRF 4.A"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): forest land](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010189) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/858

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): grassland `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010191`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): grassland](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010191)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): grassland](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010191) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'grassland' is an agriculture, forestry and land use sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Emissions and removals from rangelands and pasture land that is not considered cropland. It also includes systems with woody vegetation that fall below the threshold values used in the forest land category and are not expected to exceed them, without human intervention. The category also includes all grassland from wild lands to recreational areas as well as agricultural and silvi-pastural systems, subdivided into managed and unmanaged, consistent with national definitions."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): grassland](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010191) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): heat transfer fluid](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010366)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): heat transfer fluid](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010366) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'heat transfer fluid' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Heat transfer fluids, which include several fully fluorinated carbon compounds (either in pure form or in mixtures) with six or more carbon atoms, used and emitted during IC manufacture, testing and assembly. They are used in chillers, temperature shock testers and vapour phase reflow soldering."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): heat transfer fluid](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010366) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): integrated circuit or semiconductor `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010363`

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): integrated circuit or semiconductor](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010363) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'integrated circuit or semiconductor' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Emissions of CF4, C2F6, C3F8, c-C4F8, C4F6, C4F8O, C5F8, CHF3, CH2F2, NF3 and SF6 from uses of these gases in Integrated Circuit (IC) manufacturing in rapidly evolving ways and in varying amounts, which depend on product
(e.g., memory or logic devices) and equipment manufacturer."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): iron and steel production `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010168`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): iron and steel production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010168)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): iron and steel production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010168) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'iron and steel production' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Carbon dioxide is the predominant gas emitted from the production of iron and steel. The sources of the carbon dioxide emissions include that from carbon-containing reducing agents such as coke and pulverized coal, and, from minerals such as limestone and dolomite added."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

Lead production covers production by the sintering/smelting process as well as direct smelting. Carbon dioxide emissions result as a product of the use of a variety of carbon-based reducing agents in both production
processes."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): magnesium production `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010356`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): magnesium production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010356)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): magnesium production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010356) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'magnesium production' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Magnesium production covers GHG emissions related to both primary magnesium production as well as oxidation protection of magnesium metal during processing (recycling and casting), excluding those emissions relating to fuel use. In the primary production of magnesium, carbon dioxide (CO2) is emitted during calcination of dolomite and magnesite raw materials. Primary production of magnesium from non-carbonate raw materials does not emit carbon dioxide. In the processing of liquid magnesium, cover gases containing carbon dioxide (CO2), sulphur hexafluoride (SF6), the hydrofluorocarbon HFC 134a or the fluorinated ketone FK 5-1-12 ( C3F7C(O)C2F5) may be used. Partial
thermal decomposition and/or reaction between these compounds and liquid magnesium generates secondary compounds such as perfluorocarbons (PFCs), which are emitted in addition to unreacted cover gas constituents."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): magnesium production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010356) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): magnesium production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010356) [OEO_00010037](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010037) "CRF 2.C.4"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): magnesium production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010356) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1401
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1440

move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): magnesium production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010356) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "CRF sector (IPCC 2006): magnesium production"@en

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): manufacture of solid fuels and other energy industries `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010160`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): manufacture of solid fuels and other energy industries](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010160)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): manufacture of solid fuels and other energy industries](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010160) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'manufacture of solid fuels and other energy industries' is an energy transformation sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Combustion emissions from fuel use during the manufacture of secondary and tertiary products from solid fuels including production of charcoal."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): maritime navigation `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010202`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): maritime navigation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010202)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): maritime navigation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010202) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'maritime navigation' is a transport sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories. Emissions from fuels used by vessels of all flags that are engaged in international water-borne navigation. The international navigation may take place at sea, on inland lakes and waterways and in coastal waters. It includes emissions from journeys that depart in one country and arrive in a different country. It excludes consumption by fishing vessels."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): maritime navigation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010202) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): nitric acid production `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010345`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): nitric acid production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010345)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): nitric acid production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010345) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'nitric acid production' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Nitric acid is used as a raw material mainly in the manufacture of nitrogenous-based fertiliser. Nitric acid may also be used in the production of adipic acid and explosives (e.g., dynamite), for metal etching and in the processing of ferrous metals. The main greenhouse gas emitted from HNO3 production is nitrous oxide"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): nitric acid production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010345) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): nitric acid production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010345) [OEO_00010037](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010037) "CRF 2.B.2"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): nitric acid production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010345) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1401
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1440

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other applications `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010373`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other applications](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010373)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other applications](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010373) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'other applications' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
The properties of ozone depleting substances have made them attractive for a variety of niche applications not covered in other sub-source categories. These include electronics testing, heat transfer, dielectric fluid and medical applications. The properties of HFCs and PFCs are equally attractive in some of these sectors and they have been adopted as substitutes. There are also some historical uses of PFCs, as well as emerging use of HFCs, in these applications. These applications have leakage rates ranging from 100% emissive in year of application to around 1% per annum."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other applications](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010373) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other applications](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010373) [OEO_00010037](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010037) "CRF 2.F.6"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other applications](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010373) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1401
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1440

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other process uses of carbonates `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010343`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other process uses of carbonates](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010343)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other process uses of carbonates](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010343) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'other process uses of carbonates' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Includes limestone, dolomite and other carbonates etc. Emissions from the use of limestone, dolomite and other carbonates should be included in the industrial source category where they are emitted. Therefore, for example, where a carbonate is used as a flux for iron and steel production, resultant emissions should be reported under 2C1 &ldquo;Iron and Steel Production&rdquo; rather than this
subcategory."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other process uses of carbonates](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010343) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other product manufacture and use](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010172) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'other product manufacture and use' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Emissions of sulphur hexafluoride (SF6) and perfluorocarbons
(PFCs) from the manufacture and use of electrical equipment and a number of other products. It also includes emissions of nitrous oxide (N2O) from several products."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other transportation `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010063`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other transportation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010063)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other transportation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010063) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'other transportation' is a transport sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Combustion emissions from all remaining transport activities including pipeline transportation, ground activities in airports and harbours, and off-road activities not otherwise reported under 1 A 4 c Agriculture or 1 A 2. Manufacturing Industries and Construction. Military transport should be reported under 1 A 5."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): pipeline transport `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010064`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): pipeline transport](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010064)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): pipeline transport](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010064) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'pipeline transport' is a transport sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Combustion related emissions from the operation of pump stations and maintenance of pipelines. Transport via pipelines includes transport of gases  liquids, slurry and other commodities via pipelines. Distribution of natural or manufactured gas, water or steam from the distributor to final users is excluded and should be reported in 1 A 1 c ii or 1 A 4 a."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): refrigeration and air conditioning `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010368`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): refrigeration and air conditioning](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010368)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): refrigeration and air conditioning](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010368) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'refrigeration and air conditioning' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Refrigeration and air-conditioning systems are usually classified in six application domains or categories. These categories utilise different technologies such as heat exchangers, expansion devices, pipings and compressors. The six application domains are domestic refrigeration, commercial refrigeration, industrial processes, transport refrigeration, stationary air conditioning, mobile airconditioning systems. For all these applications, various HFCs are selectively replacing CFCs and HCFCs. For example, in developed countries, HFC-134a has replaced CFC-12 in domestic refrigeration and mobile air conditioning systems, and blends of HFCs such as R-407C (HFC-32/HFC-125/HFC-134a) and R-410A (HFC-32/HFC-125) are replacing HCFC-22 mainly in stationary air conditioning. Other, non HFC substances are used to replace CFCs and HCFCs such as iso-butane in domestic refrigeration or ammonia in industrial refrigeration. HFC- 152a is also being considered for mobile air conditioning in several regions."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): soda ash production `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010350`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): soda ash production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010350)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): soda ash production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010350) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'soda ash production' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Soda ash (sodium carbonate, Na2CO3) is a white crystalline solid that is used as a raw material in a large number of industries including glass manufacture, soap and detergents, pulp and paper production and water treatment. Emissions of CO2 from the production of soda ash vary dependent on the manufacturing process. Four different processes may be used to produce soda ash. Three of these processes, monohydrate, sodium sesquicarbonate (trona) and direct carbonation, are referred to as natural processes. The fourth, the Solvay process, is classified as a synthetic process."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): soda ash production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010350) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): soda ash production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010350) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1401

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): solvents `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010372`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): solvents](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010372)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): solvents](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010372) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'solvents' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
HFCs and, to a much lesser extent PFCs, are being used as substitutes for ozone depleting substances (most notably CFC-113). Typical HFCs used are HFC-365mfc and HFC-43-10mee. Use of these fluorinated replacements is much less widespread than the ozone depleting substances they replace. Re-capture and re-use is also much more widely practiced The primary areas of use are precision cleaning, electronics cleaning, metal cleaning and deposition applications. Emissions from aerosols containing solvents should be reported undercategory 2F4 &quot;Aerosols&quot; rather than under this category."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): solvents](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010372) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): solvents](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010372) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1401
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1440

move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): titanium dioxide production `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010349`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): titanium dioxide production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010349)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): titanium dioxide production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010349) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'titanium dioxide producion' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Titanium dioxide (TiO2) is the most important white pigment. The main use is in paint manufacture followed by paper, plastics, rubber, ceramics, fabrics, floor covering, printing ink, and other miscellaneous uses. The main production process is the chloride route, giving rise to CO2 emissions that are likely to be significant. This category also includes synthetic rutile production using the Becher process, and titanium slag production, both of which are reduction processes using fossil fuels and resulting in CO2 emissions. Synthetic rutile is the major input to TiO2 production using the chloride route."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): titanium dioxide production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010349) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): transport `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010042`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): transport](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010042)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): transport](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010042) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'transport' is a transport sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Emissions from the combustion and evaporation of fuel for all transport activity (excluding military transport), regardless of the sector, specified by sub-categories below. Emissions from fuel sold to any air or marine vessel engaged in international transport (1 A 3 a i and 1 A 3 d i) should as far as possible be excluded from the totals and subtotals in this category and should be reported separately."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): transport](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010042) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 2, Chapter 3.1, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): transport](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010042) [OEO_00010037](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010037) "CRF 1.A.3"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): transport](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010042) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/461

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): transport of CO2 `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010338`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): transport of CO2](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010338)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): transport of CO2](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010338) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'transport of CO2' is a sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
This comprises fugitive emissions from the systems used to transport captured CO2 from the source to the injection site. These emissions may comprise losses due to fugitive equipment leaks, venting and releases due to pipeline
ruptures or other accidental releases (e.g., temporary storage)."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): transport of CO2](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010338) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): waste `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010049`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): waste](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010049)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): waste](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010049) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'waste' is a waste and wastewater sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Carbon dioxide (CO2), methane (CH4) and nitrous oxide (N2O) emissions from following categories: Solid waste disposal, Biological treatment of solid waste,
Incineration and open burning of waste, Wastewater treatment and discharge."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): waste](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010049) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 5, Chapter 1.1, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): waste water treatment and discharge `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010177`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): waste water treatment and discharge](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010177)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): waste water treatment and discharge](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010177) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'waste water treatment and discharge' is a waste and wastewater sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Methane is produced from anaerobic decomposition of organic matter by bacteria in sewage facilities and from food processing and other industrial facilities during wastewater treatment. N2O is also produced by bacteria
(denitrification and nitrification) in wastewater treatment and discharge."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): waste water treatment and discharge](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010177) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): wetlands `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010192`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): wetlands](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010192)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): wetlands](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010192) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'wetlands' is an agriculture, forestry and land use sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Emissions from land that is covered or saturated by water for all or part of the year (e.g., peatland) and that does not fall into the forest land, cropland, grassland or settlements categories. The category can be subdivided into managed and unmanaged according to national definitions. It includes reservoirs as a managed sub-division and natural rivers and lakes as unmanaged sub-divisions."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): wetlands](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010192) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): wetlands](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010192) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/858
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/941

move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399

### CRF sector (IPCC 2006): zinc production `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010358`

#### Added
- Individual: [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): zinc production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010358)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): zinc production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010358) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The CRF sector (IPCC 2006) 'zinc production' is an industry sector defined by the 2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories:
Zinc production covers emissions from both primary production of zinc from ore as well as recovery of zinc from scrap metals, excluding emissions related to fuel use. Following calcination, zinc metal is produced through one of three methods; 1-electro-thermic distillation, 2-pyrometallurgical smelting or 3-electrolysis. If method 1 or 2 is used, carbon dioxide (CO2) is emitted. Method 3 does not result in carbon dioxide emissions. Recovery of zinc from metal scrap often uses the same methods as primary production and may thus produce carbon dioxide emissions, which is included in this section."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): zinc production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010358) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "2006 IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, Volume 1, Chapter 8.5, https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): zinc production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010358) [OEO_00010037](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010037) "CRF 2.C.6"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): zinc production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010358) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1401
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1440

move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): zinc production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010358) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "CRF sector (IPCC 2006): zinc production"@en

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): zinc production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010358) Type [industry sector](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000227)

- [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): zinc production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010358) [is defined by](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000504) [CRF sectors (IPCC 2006)](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000242)

### Change `https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00000004`

#### Added
- Class: [Change](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00000004)

- [Change](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00000004) [definition](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#definition) "A Process in which some independent continuant endures and 1) one or more of the dependent entities it bears increase or decrease in intensity, 2) the entity begins to bear some dependent entity or 3) the entity ceases to bear some dependent entity."@en

- [Change](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00000004) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "In CCO, the SubClassOf axioms are actually anonymous ancestors and inherited from process (BFO 2020)."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### Displacement Current `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/DisplacementCurrent`

#### Added
- Individual: [Displacement Current](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/DisplacementCurrent)

- [Displacement Current](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/DisplacementCurrent) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "&quot;Displacement Current&quot; is a quantity appearing in Maxwell's equations that is defined in terms of the rate of change of electric displacement field. Displacement current has the units of electric current density, and it has an associated magnetic field just as actual currents do. However it is not an electric current of moving charges, but a time-varying electric field. In materials, there is also a contribution from the slight motion of charges bound in atoms, dielectric polarization."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [Displacement Current](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/DisplacementCurrent) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E1L0I0M0H0T0D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E1L0I0M0H0T0D0)

- [Displacement Current](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/DisplacementCurrent) [plainTextDescription](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/plainTextDescription) "&quot;Displacement Current&quot; is a quantity appearing in Maxwell's equations that is defined in terms of the rate of change of electric displacement field. Displacement current has the units of electric current density, and it has an associated magnetic field just as actual currents do. However it is not an electric current of moving charges, but a time-varying electric field. In materials, there is also a contribution from the slight motion of charges bound in atoms, dielectric polarization."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Displacement Current](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/DisplacementCurrent) [symbol](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/symbol) "I_D"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Displacement Current](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/DisplacementCurrent) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Displacement Current"@en

- [Displacement Current](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/DisplacementCurrent) Type [QuantityKind](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/QuantityKind)


### EU allowance `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020064`

#### Added

- [EU allowance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020064) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An EU Allowance (EUA) is an emission certificate used in the European Union Emissions Trading System (EU ETS). It permits the emission of a CO2 equivalent quantity of 1 tonne."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [EU allowance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020064) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "EU-Berechtigung"@de

- [EU allowance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020064) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "EU-Klimazertifikat"@de

- [EU allowance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020064) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "EUA"@en

- [EU allowance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020064) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/677
pull request https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/740"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [EU allowance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020064) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "EU allowance"@en

- [EU allowance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020064) SubClassOf [emission certificate](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020063)

### EU emission sector division `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010130`

#### Added

### Effort Sharing Decision `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010124`

#### Added
- Individual: [Effort Sharing Decision](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010124)

- [Effort Sharing Decision](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010124) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The Effort Sharing Decision (ESD) is the European effort sharing for the years 2013-2020. It covers the European Union, Norway, Iceland and Liechtenstein and the following greenhouse gases: carbon dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide, hydrofluorocarbons, perfluorocarbons and sulphur hexafluoride. Emission quantities under the ESD are calculated using global warming potentials from the Fourth IPCC Assessment Report."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Effort Sharing Decision](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010124) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "ESD"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Effort Sharing Decision](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010124) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/834
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/857"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Effort Sharing Decision](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010124) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Effort Sharing Decision"@en

### Effort Sharing Regulation `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010125`

#### Added
- Individual: [Effort Sharing Regulation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010125)

- [Effort Sharing Regulation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010125) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The Effort Sharing Regulation (ESR) is the European effort sharing for the years 2021-2030. It covers the European Union, Norway, Iceland and Liechtenstein and the following greenhouse gases: carbon dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide, hydrofluorocarbons, perfluorocarbons, sulphur hexafluoride and nitrogen trifluoride. Emission quantities under the ESR are calculated using global warming potentials from the Fifth IPCC Assessment Report."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Effort Sharing Regulation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010125) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "ESR"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Effort Sharing Regulation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010125) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/834
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/857"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### Electric Current Phasor `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricCurrentPhasor`

#### Added
- Individual: [Electric Current Phasor](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricCurrentPhasor)

- [Electric Current Phasor](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricCurrentPhasor) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "&quot;Electric Current Phasor&quot; is a representation of current as a sinusoidal integral quantity using a complex quantity whose argument is equal to the initial phase and whose modulus is equal to the root-mean-square value. A phasor is a constant complex number, usually expressed in exponential form, representing the complex amplitude (magnitude and phase) of a sinusoidal function of time. Phasors are used by electrical engineers to simplify computations involving sinusoids, where they can often reduce a differential equation problem to an algebraic one."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [Electric Current Phasor](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricCurrentPhasor) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E1L0I0M0H0T0D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E1L0I0M0H0T0D0)

- [Electric Current Phasor](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricCurrentPhasor) [plainTextDescription](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/plainTextDescription) "&quot;Electric Current Phasor&quot; is a representation of current as a sinusoidal integral quantity using a complex quantity whose argument is equal to the initial phase and whose modulus is equal to the root-mean-square value. A phasor is a constant complex number, usually expressed in exponential form, representing the complex amplitude (magnitude and phase) of a sinusoidal function of time. Phasors are used by electrical engineers to simplify computations involving sinusoids, where they can often reduce a differential equation problem to an algebraic one."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Electric Current Phasor](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricCurrentPhasor) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Electric Current Phasor"@en

- [Electric Current Phasor](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricCurrentPhasor) Type [QuantityKind](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/QuantityKind)


### Energy per electric charge `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/EnergyPerElectricCharge`

#### Added
- Individual: [Energy per electric charge](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/EnergyPerElectricCharge)

- [Energy per electric charge](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/EnergyPerElectricCharge) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "Voltage is a representation of the electric potential energy per unit charge. If a unit of electrical charge were placed in a location, the voltage indicates the potential energy of it at that point. In other words, it is a measurement of the energy contained within an electric field, or an electric circuit, at a given point. Voltage is a scalar quantity. The SI unit of voltage is the volt, such that $1 volt = 1 joule/coulomb$."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [Energy per electric charge](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/EnergyPerElectricCharge) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E-1L2I0M1H0T-3D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E-1L2I0M1H0T-3D0)

- [Energy per electric charge](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/EnergyPerElectricCharge) [symbol](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/symbol) "V"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Energy per electric charge](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/EnergyPerElectricCharge) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Energy per electric charge"@en

- [Energy per electric charge](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/EnergyPerElectricCharge) Type [QuantityKind](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/QuantityKind)
