#### Added
- Individual: [Eurostat energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000160)

- [Eurostat energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000160) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The concept of &quot;energy balance&quot; is an accounting framework for the compilation and understanding of data on all energy products entering, exiting and being used in a country. The energy balance is the most complete statistical accounting of energy products and their flow in the economy. Columns of the energy balance represent energy products (fuels). Rows represent energy flows.

The energy balance expresses all forms of energy in a common accounting unit. The balance shows the relationships between supply, inputs to the energy transformation processes and their outputs as well as the actual energy consumption by different sectors of end-use."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Eurostat energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000160) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "This is a general description of an energy balance. A link to a complete Eurostat energy balance is provided under the URL for 'definition source'"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Eurostat energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000160) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/statistics-explained/index.php/Energy_balance#What_is_an_energy_balance.3F"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Eurostat energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000160) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "Change type and label:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1400
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1463
move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Eurostat energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000160) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Eurostat energy balance sector division"@en

- [Eurostat energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000160) Type [energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010407)


### Exchange Integral `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ExchangeIntegral`

#### Added
- Individual: [Exchange Integral](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ExchangeIntegral)

- [Exchange Integral](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ExchangeIntegral) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "&quot;Exchange Integral&quot; is the constituent of the interaction energy between the spins of adjacent electrons in matter arising from the overlap of electron state functions."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [Exchange Integral](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ExchangeIntegral) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E0L2I0M1H0T-2D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E0L2I0M1H0T-2D0)

- [Exchange Integral](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ExchangeIntegral) [plainTextDescription](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/plainTextDescription) "&quot;Exchange Integral&quot; is the constituent of the interaction energy between the spins of adjacent electrons in matter arising from the overlap of electron state functions."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Exchange Integral](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ExchangeIntegral) [symbol](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/symbol) "K"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Exchange Integral](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ExchangeIntegral) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Exchange Integral"@en

- [Exchange Integral](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ExchangeIntegral) Type [QuantityKind](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/QuantityKind)


### Expert `https://w3id.org/energy-intel/Expert`
#### Removed
- [Expert](https://w3id.org/energy-intel/Expert) [definition](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#definition) "A person who authors posts or speaks in podcast segments that evidence CanonicalMeasurementClaims about energy."@en

- [Expert](https://w3id.org/energy-intel/Expert) SubClassOf [Person](http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/Person)

#### Added
- [Expert](https://w3id.org/energy-intel/Expert) [definition](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#definition) "A foaf:Person who bears at least one ei:EnergyExpertRole. Defined (equivalent-class) so the reasoner classifies any role-bearer as an Expert. V1 redefinition supersedes V0 ``rdfs:subClassOf foaf:Person``."@en

- [Expert](https://w3id.org/energy-intel/Expert) EquivalentTo [Person](http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/Person) and ([BFO_0000053](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000053) some [energy expert role](https://w3id.org/energy-intel/EnergyExpertRole))

### German energy balance sector division `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000193`

#### Added
- Individual: [German energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000193)

- [German energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000193) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The German energy balance sector division is an energy balance used by AG Energiebilanzen (Working Group on Energy Balances) to compile the official national German energy balances."@en

- [German energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000193) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "The German Energy Balance is managed by the 'AG Energiebilanzen', where aso the energy balances can be found: https://ag-energiebilanzen.de/"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [German energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000193) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "Change type and label:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1400
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1463

move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724

New definition:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1793
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1798"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [German energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000193) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "Das Schema der Energiebilanzen umfasst eine Matrix von 33 Spalten und 68 Zeilen (einschlie&szlig;lich der Summenspalten und -zeilen). In der horizontalen Gliederung (Spalten) werden die Energietr&auml;ger ausgewiesen, die der energetischen und nichtenergetischen Nutzung dienen. In der vertikalen Gliederung (Zeilen) werden f&uuml;r die jeweiligen Energietr&auml;ger Aufkommen, Umwandlung und Verwendung erfasst.

Als Energietr&auml;ger werden alle Quellen oder Stoffe bezeichnet, in denen Energie mechanisch, thermisch, chemisch oder physikalisch gespeichert ist. F&uuml;nf Kerngruppe werden bei der Energiebilanz unterschieden:

    Fossile Brennstoffe
    Erneuerbare Energien
    Strom
    Kernbrennstoffe
    Fernw&auml;rme

Bilanzebenen
Schematische Darstellung des Bilanzraums der deutschen Arbeitsgemeinschaft Energiebilanzen
Prim&auml;r- und Endenergie sowie Umwandlungsverluste

Die Abgrenzung erfolgt in drei Bilanzbereichen:

    die Prim&auml;renergiebilanz,
    die Umwandlungsbilanz und
    der Endenergieverbrauch.

In der Prim&auml;renergiebilanz werden die Energietr&auml;ger mit ihrem Mengenaufkommen bei der Gewinnung und Anlieferung nach folgenden Kriterien erfasst:

    inl&auml;ndische Gewinnung von Energietr&auml;gern,
    Au&szlig;enhandel mit Energietr&auml;gern, unterteilt nach Einfuhr und Ausfuhr,
    Hochseebunkerungen (Heiz&ouml;l, Dieselkraftstoff und Schmierstoffe f&uuml;r die nationale und internationale Seeschifffahrt in deutschen H&auml;fen. Ohne Lieferungen an Binnen- und K&uuml;stenmotorschiffe und Fischerei, die zum Sektor Verkehr (Endenergieverbrauch) z&auml;hlen.)
    Bestandsver&auml;nderungen, getrennt erfasst nach Bestandsentnahmen und -aufstockungen"@de
  - [source](http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/source) "https://de.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Energiebilanz_(Energiewirtschaft)&amp;oldid=176259115"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [German energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000193) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "German energy balance sector division"@en

- [German energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000193) Type [energy balance sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010407)

### Gram `http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/GM`

#### Added
- Individual: [Gram](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/GM)

- [Gram](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/GM) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "A unit of mass in the metric system. The name comes from the Greek gramma, a small weight identified in later Roman and Byzantine times with the Latin scripulum or scruple (the English scruple is equal to about 1.3 grams). The gram was originally defined to be the mass of one cubic centimeter of pure water, but to provide precise standards it was necessary to construct physical objects of specified mass. One gram is now defined to be 1/1000 of the mass of the standard kilogram, a platinum-iridium bar carefully guarded by the International Bureau of Weights and Measures in Paris for more than a century. (The kilogram, rather than the gram, is considered the base unit of mass in the SI.) The gram is a small mass, equal to about 15.432 grains or 0.035 273 966 ounce. "^^[HTML](http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#HTML)

- [Gram](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/GM) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-EMU](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-EMU)

- [Gram](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/GM) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-GAUSS](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-GAUSS)

- [Gram](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/GM) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS)

- [Gram](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/GM) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [SI](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/SI)

- [Gram](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/GM) [conversionMultiplier](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/conversionMultiplier) 0.001

- [Gram](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/GM) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E0L0I0M1H0T0D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E0L0I0M1H0T0D0)

- [Gram](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/GM) [symbol](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/symbol) "g"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Gram](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/GM) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Gram"@en

### Joule `http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/J`

#### Added
- Individual: [Joule](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/J)

- [Joule](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/J) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "$\textit{Joule}$ is the SI unit of work or energy, defined to be the work done by a force of one newton acting to move an object through a distance of one meter in the direction in which the force is applied.
  Equivalently, since kinetic energy is one half the mass times the square of the velocity, one joule is the kinetic energy of a mass of two kilograms moving at a velocity of $1 m/s$.
  "^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [Joule](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/J) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-EMU](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-EMU)

- [Joule](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/J) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-GAUSS](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-GAUSS)

- [Joule](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/J) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS)

- [Joule](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/J) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [SI](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/SI)

- [Joule](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/J) [conversionMultiplier](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/conversionMultiplier) 1.0

- [Joule](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/J) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E0L2I0M1H0T-2D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E0L2I0M1H0T-2D0)

- [Joule](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/J) [plainTextDescription](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/plainTextDescription) "The SI unit of work or energy, defined to be the work done by a force of one newton acting to move an object through a distance of one meter in the direction in which the force is applied. Equivalently, since kinetic energy is one half the mass times the square of the velocity, one joule is the kinetic energy of a mass of two kilograms moving at a velocity of 1 m/s. This is the same as 107 ergs in the CGS system, or approximately 0.737 562 foot-pound in the traditional English system. In other energy units, one joule equals about 9.478 170 x 10-4 Btu, 0.238 846 (small) calories, or 2.777 778 x 10-4 watt hour. The joule is named for the British physicist James Prescott Joule (1818-1889), who demonstrated the equivalence of mechanical and thermal energy in a famous experiment in 1843. "^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Joule](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/J) [symbol](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/symbol) "J"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### KSG sector agriculture `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010069`

#### Added
- Individual: [KSG sector agriculture](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010069)

- [KSG sector agriculture](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010069) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The KSG sector agriculture is an agriculture, forestry and land use sector defined by the KSG sector division. It is an aggregate of the following two sectors:
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): agriculture, forestry and fishing (CRF 1.A.4.c)
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): agriculture (CRF 3)"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [KSG sector agriculture](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010069) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "KSG-Sektor Landwirtschaft"@de

- [KSG sector agriculture](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010069) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/580
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/595

move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [KSG sector agriculture](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010069) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "KSG sector agriculture"@en

### KSG sector buildings `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010067`

#### Added
- Individual: [KSG sector buildings](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010067)

- [KSG sector buildings](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010067) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The KSG sector buildings is a building sector defined by the KSG sector division. It is an aggregate of the following two sectors:
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): commercial and institutional (CRF 1.A.4.a)
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): residential (CRF 1.A.4.b)
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): fuel combustion - other"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [KSG sector buildings](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010067) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "KSG-Sektor Geb&auml;ude"@de

pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### KSG sector energy industry `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010065`

#### Added
- Individual: [KSG sector energy industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010065)

- [KSG sector energy industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010065) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The KSG sector energy industry is an energy transformation sector defined by the KSG sector division. It is an aggregate of the following three sectors:
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): energy industry (CRF 1.A.1.a)
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): other transportation (CRF 1.A.3.e)
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): fugitive emissions from fuels (CRF 1.B)"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [KSG sector energy industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010065) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "KSG-Sektor Energiewirtschaft"@de

- [KSG sector energy industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010065) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "Bundes-Klimaschutzgesetz (KSG), Anlage 1 (zu den &sect;&sect; 4 und 5) https://www.gesetze-im-internet.de/ksg/anlage_1.html"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [KSG sector energy industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010065) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/580

move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [KSG sector energy industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010065) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "KSG sector energy industry"@en

- [KSG sector energy industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010065) Type [energy transformation sector](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000158)

- [KSG sector energy industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010065) [is defined by](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000504) [KSG sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010056)

- [KSG sector energy industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010065) [has subsector](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010467) [CRF sector (IPCC 2006): energy industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010040)

### KSG sector industry `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010066`

#### Added
- Individual: [KSG sector industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010066)

- [KSG sector industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010066) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The KSG sector industry is an industry sector defined by the KSG sector division. It is an aggregate of the following three sectors:
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): manufacturing industries and construction (CRF 1.A.2)
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): CO2 transport and storage (CRF 1.C)
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): industrial processes and product use (CRF 2)"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [KSG sector industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010066) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "KSG-Sektor Industrie"@de

- [KSG sector industry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010066) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "Bundes-Klimaschutzgesetz (KSG), Anlage 1 (zu den &sect;&sect; 4 und 5) https://www.gesetze-im-internet.de/ksg/anlage_1.html"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### KSG sector land use, land-use change and forestry `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010071`

#### Added
- Individual: [KSG sector land use, land-use change and forestry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010071)

- [KSG sector land use, land-use change and forestry](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010071) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The KSG sector land use, land-use change and forestry is an agriculture, forestry and land use sector defined by the KSG sector division.
It is equivalent to the CRF sector (IPCC 2006): land use, land-use change and forestry (CRF 4)."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### KSG sector transport `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010068`

#### Added
- Individual: [KSG sector transport](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010068)

- [KSG sector transport](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010068) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The KSG sector transport is a transport sector defined by the KSG sector division. It is an aggregate of the following four sectors:
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): domestic aviation (CRF 1.A.3.a)
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): road transportation (CRF 1.A.3.b)
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): railways (CRF 1.A.3.c)
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): domestic navigation (CRF 1.A.3.d)"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [KSG sector transport](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010068) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "KSG-Sektor Verkehr"@de

pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/595

move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### Keupayaan elektrik `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricPotential`

#### Added
- Individual: [Keupayaan elektrik](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricPotential)

- [Keupayaan elektrik](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricPotential) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "The Electric Potential is a scalar valued quantity associated with an electric field. The electric potential $\phi(x)$ at a point, $x$, is formally defined as the line integral of the electric field taken along a path from x to the point at infinity. If the electric field is static, that is time independent, then the choice of the path is arbitrary; however if the electric field is time dependent, taking the integral a different paths will produce different results."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [Keupayaan elektrik](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricPotential) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E-1L2I0M1H0T-3D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E-1L2I0M1H0T-3D0)

### Kilowatt `http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloW`

#### Added
- Individual: [Kilowatt](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloW)

- [Kilowatt](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloW) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "
  The unit $\textit{Kilowatt}$ is a derived unit of power in the International System of Units (SI).
  The unit, defined as 1,000 joule per second, measures the rate of energy conversion or transfer.
  "^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

### Kilowatthour `http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloW-HR`

#### Added
- Individual: [Kilowatthour](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloW-HR)

- [Kilowatthour](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloW-HR) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "The kilowatt hour, or kilowatt-hour, (symbol $kW \cdot h$, $kW h$ or $kWh$) is a unit of energy equal to 1000 watt hours or 3.6 megajoules. For constant power, energy in watt hours is the product of power in watts and time in hours. The kilowatt hour is most commonly known as a billing unit for energy delivered to consumers by electric utilities."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [Kilowatthour](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloW-HR) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-EMU](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-EMU)

### Level Width `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/LevelWidth`

#### Added
- Individual: [Level Width](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/LevelWidth)

- [Level Width](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/LevelWidth) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "The &quot;Level Width&quot; is the uncertainty in the energy of a quantum-mechanical system having discrete energy levels in a state that is not strictly stationary. The system may be an atom, a molecule, or an atomic nucleus."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

The system may be an atom, a molecule, or an atomic nucleus."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Level Width](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/LevelWidth) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Level Width"@en

### Magnetomotive Force `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/MagnetomotiveForce`

#### Added
- Individual: [Magnetomotive Force](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/MagnetomotiveForce)

- [Magnetomotive Force](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/MagnetomotiveForce) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "
$\text{Magnetomotive Force}$, also referred to as ($mmf$), is the ability of an electric circuit to produce magnetic flux.
  Just as the ability of a battery to produce electric current is called its electromotive force
  or emf, mmf is taken as the work required to move a unit magnet pole from any point through any path
  which links the electric circuit back the same point in the presence of the magnetic force produced
  by the electric current in the circuit.
$\text{Magnetomotive Force}$ is the scalar line integral of the magnetic field strength along a closed path.
  "^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [Magnetomotive Force](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/MagnetomotiveForce) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E1L0I0M0H0T0D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E1L0I0M0H0T0D0)

- [Magnetomotive Force](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/MagnetomotiveForce) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Magnetomotive Force"@en

- [Magnetomotive Force](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/MagnetomotiveForce) Type [QuantityKind](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/QuantityKind)


### Mass per Energy `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/MassPerEnergy`

- Individual: [Mass per Energy](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/MassPerEnergy)

- [Mass per Energy](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/MassPerEnergy) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "Mass per Energy ($m/E$) is a physical quantity that bridges mass and energy. The SI unit is $kg/J$ or equivalently $s^2/m^2$."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [Mass per Energy](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/MassPerEnergy) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E0L-2I0M0H0T2D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E0L-2I0M0H0T2D0)

- [Mass per Energy](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/MassPerEnergy) [plainTextDescription](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/plainTextDescription) "Mass per Energy is a physical quantity that can be used to relate the energy of a system to its mass."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Mass per Energy](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/MassPerEnergy) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Mass per Energy"@en

- [Mass per Energy](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/MassPerEnergy) Type [QuantityKind](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/QuantityKind)

### Metric Ton `http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/TON_Metric`

#### Added
- Individual: [Metric Ton](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/TON_Metric)

- [Metric Ton](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/TON_Metric) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "The tonne (SI unit symbol: t) is a metric system unit of mass equal to 1,000 kilograms (2,204.6 pounds). It is a non-SI unit accepted for use with SI. To avoid confusion with the ton, it is also known as the metric tonne and metric ton in the United States[3] and occasionally in the United Kingdom. In SI units and prefixes, the tonne is a megagram (Mg), a rarely-used symbol, easily confused with mg, for milligram."^^[HTML](http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#HTML)

- [Metric Ton](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/TON_Metric) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-EMU](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-EMU)

- [Metric Ton](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/TON_Metric) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-GAUSS](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-GAUSS)

- [Metric Ton](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/TON_Metric) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS)

- [Metric Ton](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/TON_Metric) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [SI](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/SI)

- [Metric Ton](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/TON_Metric) [conversionMultiplier](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/conversionMultiplier) 1000.0

- [Metric Ton](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/TON_Metric) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E0L0I0M1H0T0D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E0L0I0M1H0T0D0)

- [Metric Ton](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/TON_Metric) [symbol](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/symbol) "t"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Metric Ton](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/TON_Metric) [alternative label](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#altLabel) "metric-tonne"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

(https://unfccc.int/sites/default/files/resource/cp2019_13a01_adv.pdf, 6/CP.25, VI, Table 2)"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### NC/BR sector: energy `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010305`

#### Added
- Individual: [NC/BR sector: energy](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010305)

- [NC/BR sector: energy](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010305) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The NC/BR sector &quot;energy&quot; is a sector defined by an NC/BR sector division that includes all energy use that is not related to transport or industry. The sector includes:
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): energy industry
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): fuel combustion - other sectors
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): fuel combustion - other
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): fugitive emissions from fuels
- CRF sector (IPCC 2006): CO2 transport and storage"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [NC/BR sector: energy](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010305) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1273
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1317

Improve definition and axioms:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1449
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1462

move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [NC/BR sector: energy](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010305) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "NC/BR sector: energy"@en

- [NC/BR sector: energy](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010305) Type [sector](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000367)

- [NC/BR sector: energy](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010305) Type [is defined by](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000504) some [NC/BR sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010304)

-  DifferentIndividuals: [NC/BR sector: energy](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010305), [NC/BR sector: transport](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010306), [NC/BR sector: industry/industrial processes and product use](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010307), [NC/BR sector: agriculture](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010308), [NC/BR sector: forestry/LULUCF](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010309), [NC/BR sector: waste management/waste](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010310), [NC/BR sector: other](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010311)

### Opening Ratio `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/OpeningRatio`

#### Added
- Individual: [Opening Ratio](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/OpeningRatio)

- [Opening Ratio](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/OpeningRatio) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "In the context of mechanical systems, &quot;opening ratio&quot; might refer to the proportion of time
  or the extent to which a valve or gate is open relative to its maximum capacity."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Opening Ratio](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/OpeningRatio) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E0L0I0M0H0T0D1](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E0L0I0M0H0T0D1)

- [Opening Ratio](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/OpeningRatio) [plainTextDescription](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/plainTextDescription) "In the context of mechanical systems, &quot;opening ratio&quot; might refer to the proportion of time
  or the extent to which a valve or gate is open relative to its maximum capacity."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Opening Ratio](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/OpeningRatio) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Opening Ratio"@en

### Performance Specification `https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00000127`

#### Added
- Class: [Performance Specification](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00000127)

- [Performance Specification](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00000127) [definition](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#definition) "A Directive Information Content Entity that prescribes some aspect of the behavior of a participant in a Process given one or more operating conditions."@en

issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/2107
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/2108"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Performance Specification](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00000127) [ont00001760](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001760) "https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/InformationEntityOntology"^^[anyURI](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI)

- [Performance Specification](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00000127) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "The CCO also assigns this class the following subclass axiom: 'describes condition' some entity
However, this was left out."@en

- [Performance Specification](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00000127) [isDefinedBy](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#isDefinedBy) [cco-extracted.owl](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/imports/cco-extracted.owl)

- [Performance Specification](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00000127) SubClassOf [directive information entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000033)

#### Added

pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1991"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### Reflectance `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Reflectance`

#### Added
- Individual: [Reflectance](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Reflectance)

- [Reflectance](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Reflectance) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "Reflectance generally refers to the fraction of incident power that is reflected at an interface, while the term &quot;reflection coefficient&quot; is used for the fraction of electric field reflected. Reflectance is always a real number between zero and 1.0."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [Reflectance](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Reflectance) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E0L0I0M0H0T0D1](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E0L0I0M0H0T0D1)

- [Reflectance](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Reflectance) [plainTextDescription](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/plainTextDescription) "Reflectance generally refers to the fraction of incident power that is reflected at an interface, while the term &quot;reflection coefficient&quot; is used for the fraction of electric field reflected. Reflectance is always a real number between zero and 1.0."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Reflectance](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Reflectance) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Reflectance"@en

- [Reflectance](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Reflectance) Type [QuantityKind](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/QuantityKind)

- [Reflectance](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Reflectance) [has broader](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#broader) [Dimensionless Ratio](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/DimensionlessRatio)


### Relative Humidity `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeHumidity`

#### Added
- Individual: [Relative Humidity](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeHumidity)

- [Relative Humidity](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeHumidity) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "
  $\textit{Relative Humidity}$ is the ratio of the partial pressure of water vapor in an air-water mixture to the saturated vapor pressure of water at a prescribed temperature.
  The relative humidity of air depends not only on temperature but also on the pressure of the system of interest.
  $$$$
  $\textit{Relative Humidity}$ is also referred to as $\textit{Relative Partial Pressure}$.
  $$$$
  Relative partial pressure is often referred to as $RH$ and expressed in percent.
  "^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [Relative Humidity](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeHumidity) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E0L0I0M0H0T0D1](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E0L0I0M0H0T0D1)

- [Relative Humidity](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeHumidity) [alternative label](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#altLabel) "RH"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Relative Humidity](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeHumidity) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Relative Humidity"@en

- [Relative Humidity](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeHumidity) Type [QuantityKind](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/QuantityKind)

- [Relative Humidity](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeHumidity) [has broader](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#broader) [Relative Partial Pressure](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativePartialPressure)


### Relative Luminous Flux `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeLuminousFlux`

#### Added
- Individual: [Relative Luminous Flux](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeLuminousFlux)

- [Relative Luminous Flux](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeLuminousFlux) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "Relative Luminous Flux or Relative Luminous Power is the measure of the perceived power of light. It differs from radiant flux, the measure of the total power of light emitted, in that luminous flux is adjusted to reflect the varying sensitivity of the human eye to different wavelengths of light. It is expressed as a percentage or fraction of full power."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [Relative Luminous Flux](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeLuminousFlux) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E0L0I0M0H0T0D1](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E0L0I0M0H0T0D1)

- [Relative Luminous Flux](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeLuminousFlux) [plainTextDescription](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/plainTextDescription) "Relative Luminous Flux or Relative Luminous Power is the measure of the perceived power of light. It differs from radiant flux, the measure of the total power of light emitted, in that luminous flux is adjusted to reflect the varying sensitivity of the human eye to different wavelengths of light. It is expressed as a percentage or fraction of full power."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Relative Luminous Flux](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeLuminousFlux) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Relative Luminous Flux"@en

- [Relative Luminous Flux](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeLuminousFlux) Type [QuantityKind](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/QuantityKind)

- [Relative Luminous Flux](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/RelativeLuminousFlux) [has broader](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#broader) [Dimensionless Ratio](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/DimensionlessRatio)

#### Added
- Individual: [Renewable Energy Directive sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000355)

pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1446

issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1449
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1462

move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Renewable Energy Directive sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000355) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Renewable Energy Directive sector division"@en

### Thermal Energy `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ThermalEnergy`

#### Added
- Individual: [Thermal Energy](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ThermalEnergy)

- [Thermal Energy](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ThermalEnergy) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "&quot;Thermal Energy&quot; is the portion of the thermodynamic or internal energy of a system that is responsible for the temperature of the system. From a macroscopic thermodynamic description, the thermal energy of a system is given by its constant volume specific heat capacity C(T), a temperature coefficient also called thermal capacity, at any given absolute temperature (T): $U_{thermal} = C(T) \cdot T$."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [Thermal Energy](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ThermalEnergy) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E0L2I0M1H0T-2D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E0L2I0M1H0T-2D0)

- [Thermal Energy](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ThermalEnergy) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Thermal Energy"@en

- [Thermal Energy](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ThermalEnergy) Type [QuantityKind](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/QuantityKind)

- [Thermal Energy](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ThermalEnergy) [has broader](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#broader) [energie](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Energy)

### Voltage `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Voltage`

#### Added
- Individual: [Voltage](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Voltage)

- [Voltage](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Voltage) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "$\textit{Voltage}$, also referred to as $\textit{Electric Tension}$, is the difference between electrical potentials of two points. For an electric field within a medium, $U_{ab} = - \int_{r_a}^{r_b} E . {dr}$, where $E$ is electric field strength.
For an irrotational electric field, the voltage is independent of the path between the two points $a$ and $b$."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [Voltage](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Voltage) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E-1L2I0M1H0T-3D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E-1L2I0M1H0T-3D0)

- [Voltage](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Voltage) [symbol](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/symbol) "U"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [Voltage](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Voltage) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Voltage"@en

- [Voltage](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Voltage) Type [QuantityKind](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/QuantityKind)

### Volume `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Volume`

#### Added
- Individual: [Volume](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Volume)

- [Volume](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Volume) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "The volume of a solid object is the three-dimensional concept of how much space it occupies, often quantified numerically. One-dimensional figures (such as lines) and two-dimensional shapes (such as squares) are assigned zero volume in the three-dimensional space."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

One-dimensional figures (such as lines) and two-dimensional shapes (such as squares) are assigned zero volume in the three-dimensional space."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### active `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000049`

#### Added
- Individual: [active](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000049)

- [active](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000049) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "active"@en

### agent `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000051`

#### Added
- Class: [agent](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000051)

- [agent](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000051) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Agent is a role of a person or organisation that directs its activity towards achieving goals."@en

- [agent](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000051) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Agent"@de

- [agent](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000051) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Agentin"@de

- [agent](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000051) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue:https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/148
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/210

add 'has bearer' axiom
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1305
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1310

replaced 'has bearer' with 'characteristic of'
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1907
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1928"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [agent](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000051) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "agent"@en


### aggregation type `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140068`

#### Added
- Class: [aggregation type](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140068)

- [aggregation type](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140068) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "aggregation of a data set with spatial references"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [aggregation type](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140068) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "temporal aggregation of continuous measurements for a discrete time series"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [aggregation type](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140068) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An aggregation type is a data descriptor that contains information on the aggregation method applied on a data set."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [aggregation type](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140068) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Aggregationsart"@de

- [aggregation type](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140068) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "add axiom `'is about' some 'typical period'`:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/880
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/883

issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/571
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/610"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [aggregation type](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140068) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "aggregation type"@en

- [aggregation type](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140068) SubClassOf [data descriptor](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000119)

- [aggregation type](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140068) SubClassOf [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) some [typical period](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020089)


### agriculture, forestry and land use sector `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010035`

#### Added
- Class: [agriculture, forestry and land use sector](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010035)

- [agriculture, forestry and land use sector](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010035) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A agriculture, forestry and land use (AFOLU) sector is a sector that covers activities and natural processes from agriculture, forestry, land use and land use change."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [agriculture, forestry and land use sector](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010035) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "AFOLU sector"@en

- [agriculture, forestry and land use sector](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010035) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Landwirtschaft, Forstwirtschaft und Landnutzungssektor"@de

- [agriculture, forestry and land use sector](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010035) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/30
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/484

move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [agriculture, forestry and land use sector](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010035) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "agriculture, forestry and land use sector"@en

- [agriculture, forestry and land use sector](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010035) SubClassOf [sector](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000367)


### aircraft `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010292`

#### Added
- Class: [aircraft](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010292)

- [aircraft](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010292) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An aircraft is a vehicle that is flying in the air."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [aircraft](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010292) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Luftfahrzeug"@de

- [aircraft](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010292) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1277
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1293"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [aircraft](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010292) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "aircraft"@en

- [aircraft](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010292) SubClassOf [vehicle](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010023)


### ambiguous uncertainty `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400014`

#### Added
- Individual: [ambiguous uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400014)

- [ambiguous uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400014) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Ambiguous uncertainty is a nature of model uncertainty that stems from multiple frames of reference about a phenomenon existing similtaneously."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [ambiguous uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400014) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "ambiguity of uncertainty"@en

- [ambiguous uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400014) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/264816512_Classifying_and_communicating_uncertainties_in_model-based_policy_analysis"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [ambiguous uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400014) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1829
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1945"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [ambiguous uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400014) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "ambiguous uncertainty"@en

- [ambiguous uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400014) Type [nature of model uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400013)


### analysis scope `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020072`

#### Added
- Class: [analysis scope](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020072)

- [analysis scope](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020072) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An analysis scope is an information content entity that describes the boundaries of what a study or scenario covers."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [analysis scope](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020072) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Analyseumfang"@de

- [analysis scope](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020072) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/638
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/764
Add 'is about' axioms:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1059
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1286
rework module structure
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1592
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1652"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [analysis scope](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020072) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "analysis scope"@en

- [analysis scope](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020072) SubClassOf [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030)

- [analysis scope](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020072) SubClassOf [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) some [sector](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000367)

- [analysis scope](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020072) SubClassOf [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) some [methodical focus](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020018)

- [analysis scope](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020072) SubClassOf [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) some [study region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020032)

- [analysis scope](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020072) SubClassOf [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) some [considered region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020035)

- [analysis scope](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020072) SubClassOf [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) some [scenario year](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020097)

- [analysis scope](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020072) SubClassOf [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) some [scenario horizon](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020098)


### annual `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020161`

### carbon dioxide `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000006`

#### Added
- Class: [carbon dioxide](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000006)

- [carbon dioxide](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000006) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Carbon dioxide is a portion of matter with the chemical formula CO2. It has a gaseous normal state of matter. It occurs naturally in the atmosphere as a trace gas and can work as a greenhouse gas."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### cement `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240029`

#### Added
- Class: [cement](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240029)

- [cement](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240029) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Cement is a portion of matter that is made from limestone and clay; other elements may be present. It is used as a building material to set as a solid mass or is used as an ingredient in making mortar or concrete. It has a solid normal state of matter. It is the physical output of an industrial process and has a commodity role."@en

- [cement](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240029) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Zement"@de

### chemical substance `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240027`

#### Added
- Class: [chemical substance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240027)

- [chemical substance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240027) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A chemical substance is a portion of matter of constant composition and that is neither a metal or mineral. It is composed of molecular entities of the same type or of different types that is used in or produced by a chemical reaction involving changes to atoms or molecules. It is the physical output of an industrial process and has a commodity role."@en

rework module structure

pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1652"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [chemical substance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240027) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "chemical substance"@en

### considered region `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020035`

#### Added
- Class: [considered region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020035)

- [considered region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020035) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "In a study, the spatial focus of the investigation lies on one European country, e.g. Germany. This is the study region.
The study region is not modelled as a whole, but several subregions are modelled, e.g. German Federal States. These are study subregions.
In the study, a spatial region outside the study region plays a role. E.g. for modelling the electricity transfer between Germany and its neighbouring countries. In this example, the neighbouring countries represent the interacting / external region.
The union of the study region and the interacting/ external region ist the considered region."@en

- [considered region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020035) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A considered region is a region of relevance that has the considered region role."@en

- [considered region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020035) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "betrachtete Region"@de

- [considered region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020035) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/475
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/585

rework module structure
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1592
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1652

relocated, made equivalent
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1745
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1749"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [considered region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020035) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "considered region"@en

- [considered region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020035) SubClassOf [region of relevance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00360020)


### considered region role `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00360022`

#### Added
- Class: [considered region role](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00360022)

- [considered region role](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00360022) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "In a study, the spatial focus of the investigation lies on one European country, e.g. Germany. This is the study region.
The study region is not modelled as a whole, but several subregions are modelled, e.g. German Federal States. These are study subregions.
In the study, a spatial region outside the study region plays a role. E.g. for modelling the electricity transfer between Germany and its neighbouring countries. In this example, the neighbouring countries represent the interacting / external region.
The union of the study region and the interacting/ external region ist the considered region."@en

- [considered region role](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00360022) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A considered region role is the role of a region of relevance that is used in an analysis."@en

### crude oil `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000115`

#### Added
- Class: [crude oil](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000115)

- [crude oil](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000115) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Crude oil is a portion of mater of fossil origin comprising a mixture of hydrocarbons and associated impurities, such as sulphur. It exists in the liquid phase under normal surface temperature and pressure and its physical characteristics (density, viscosity, etc.) are highly variable. This category includes field or lease condensate recovered from associated and non-associated gas where it is commingled with the commercial crude oil stream."@en

- [crude oil](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000115) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Roh&ouml;l"@de

- [crude oil](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000115) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "petroleum"@en

- [crude oil](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000115) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "REGULATION (EC) No 1099/2008 OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL of 22 October 2008 on energy statistics https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:32008R1099&amp;from=EN"@en

- [crude oil](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000115) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "make direct subclass of 'portion of matter' and preserve axioms from old class 'oil and petroleum products'
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/811
https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1024

Add petroleum as alternative label:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1318
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1331

rework module structure
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1592
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1652"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [crude oil](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000115) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "crude oil"@en

- [crude oil](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000115) SubClassOf [portion of matter](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000331)

### currency `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00040004`

- [currency](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00040004) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "The currency used in France in 2020 was Euro."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [currency](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00040004) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A unit which is a measure of the medium of an exchange value, defined by reference to the geographical location of the monetary authorities responsible for it."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### data entity `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027`

#### Added
- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [editor preferred term](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000111) "data item"@en

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An information content entity that is intended to be a truthful statement about something (modulo, e.g., measurement precision or other systematic errors) and is constructed/acquired by a method which reliably tends to produce (approximately) truthful statements."@en

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "JAR: datum     -- well, this will be very tricky to define, but maybe some
information-like stuff that might be put into a computer and that is
meant, by someone, to denote and/or to be interpreted by some
process... I would include lists, tables, sentences... I think I might
defer to Barry, or to Brian Cantwell Smith

JAR: A data item is an approximately justified approximately true approximate belief"@en

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "http://www.ontobee.org/ontology/IAO?iri=http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A data item is an information content entity that is intended to be a truthful statement about something (modulo, e.g., measurement precision or other systematic errors) and is constructed/acquired by a method which reliably tends to produce (approximately) truthful statements."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)
  - [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/823
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/826"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [isDefinedBy](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#isDefinedBy) [iao-extracted.owl](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/imports/iao-extracted.owl)

- [data entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000027) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "data item"@en

pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1326"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### deep uncertainty `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400020`

#### Added
- Individual: [deep uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400020)

- [deep uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400020) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Deep uncertainty is a level of model uncertainty at which one can enumerate multiple alternatives, without being able to rank them in order of likelihood."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [deep uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400020) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/264816512_Classifying_and_communicating_uncertainties_in_model-based_policy_analysis"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [deep uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400020) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1829
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1945"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [deep uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400020) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "deep uncertainty"@en

- [deep uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400020) Type [level of model uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400017)

### effort sharing `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010123`

#### Added
- Class: [effort sharing](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010123)

- [effort sharing](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010123) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Effort sharing is a regulatory instrument that sets a common emission reduction goal for a number of countries. Each country has a national reduction goal. Under- or overachievement of that goal can be compensated by various flexibilities given in the regulation."@en

- [effort sharing](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010123) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Lastenteilung"@de

- [effort sharing](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010123) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/834
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/857

Make subclass of 'regulatory instrument' and improve definition:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1545
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1786"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [effort sharing](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010123) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "effort sharing"@en

- [effort sharing](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010123) SubClassOf [regulatory instrument](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010458)

- [effort sharing](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010123) SubClassOf [uses](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000503) some [emission certificate](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020063)

### elektromos áramerősség `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricCurrent`

#### Added
- Individual: [elektromos áramerősség](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricCurrent)

- [elektromos áramerősség](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricCurrent) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "
  The quantity kind $\textit{Electric Current}$ is the flow (movement) of electric charge.
  The amount of electric current through some surface, for example, a section through a copper conductor,
   is defined as the amount of electric charge flowing through that surface over time.
  Current is a scalar-valued quantity.
  Electric current is one of the base quantities in the International System of Quantities, ISQ,
   on which the International System of Units, SI, is based.
  "^^[HTML](http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#HTML)

- [elektromos áramerősség](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/ElectricCurrent) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E1L0I0M0H0T0D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E1L0I0M0H0T0D0)

The amount of electric current through some surface, for example, a section through a copper conductor, is defined as the amount of electric charge flowing through that surface over time. Current is a scalar-valued quantity. Electric current is one of the base quantities in the International System of Quantities, ISQ, on which the International System of Units, SI, is based. "^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### energie `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Energy`

#### Added

### energy balance data set `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010404`

change label

### energy transfer `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020103`

#### Added
- Class: [energy transfer](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020103)

- [energy transfer](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020103) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Energy transfer is an energy transformation in form of a spatial transmission, that does not change the types of energies, apart from losses, e.g. waste heat."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [energy transfer](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020103) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "energy transfer"@en

- [energy transfer](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020103) SubClassOf [energy transformation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020003)

### energy use `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010210`

#### Added
- Class: [energy use](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010210)

- [energy use](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010210) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Energy use is the consumption of an energy carrier making use of the energy it carries."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### frecvență `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Frequency`

#### Added
- Individual: [frecvență](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Frequency)

- [frecvență](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Frequency) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "&quot;Frequency&quot; is the number of occurrences of a repeating event per unit time. The repetition of the events may be periodic (that is. the length of time between event repetitions is fixed) or aperiodic (i.e. the length of time between event repetitions varies). Therefore, we distinguish between periodic and aperiodic frequencies. In the SI system, periodic frequency is measured in hertz (Hz) or multiples of hertz, while aperiodic frequency is measured in becquerel (Bq).  In spectroscopy, $\nu$ is mostly used. Light passing through different media keeps its frequency, but not its wavelength or wavenumber."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [frecvență](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Frequency) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E0L0I0M0H0T-1D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E0L0I0M0H0T-1D0)

- [frecvență](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Frequency) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Frekuensi"@ms

- [frecvență](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Frequency) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Frekvence"@cs

- [frecvență](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Frequency) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Frequenz"@de
