### fuel `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000173`

#### Added
- Class: [fuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000173)

- [fuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000173) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A fuel is a portion of matter that has the disposition to be an energy carrier and that has a fuel role."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [fuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000173) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Brennstoff"@de

- [fuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000173) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/227
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/279
add role fuel commodity
https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/748

shorten definition:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1184
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1187

Add good role:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1269
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1299

move to oeo-shared:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1384
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1390

rework module structure
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1592
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1652"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [fuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000173) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "fuel"@en

- [fuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000173) SubClassOf [portion of matter](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000331)


### fuel production `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010420`

#### Added
- Class: [fuel production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010420)

- [fuel production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010420) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Fuel production is a production process which has fuel as physical output."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [fuel production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010420) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1526
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1575"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [fuel production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010420) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "fuel production"@en

- [fuel production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010420) EquivalentTo [production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240003) and ([has physical output](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000533) some [fuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000173))

- [fuel production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010420) SubClassOf [production](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240003)


### fuel role `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000001`

#### Added
- Class: [fuel role](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000001)

- [fuel role](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000001) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A fuel role is a role of a portion of matter that has the disposition to be an energy carrier and is used in a process that releases the carried energy by transforming the portion of matter into a different kind of portion of matter in a way that releases heat or does work."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [fuel role](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000001) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/227
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/279

add has bearer axiom:

pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1178

move to oeo-shared:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1384
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1390

rework module structure
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1592
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1652

replaced 'has bearer' with 'characteristic of'
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1907
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1928"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [fuel role](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000001) [may be identical to](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0006011) "http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_33292"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)
  - [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/636

- [fuel role](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000001) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "fuel role"@en


### fuel-powered electricity generation process `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050001`

#### Added
- Class: [fuel-powered electricity generation process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050001)

- [fuel-powered electricity generation process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050001) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A fuel-powered electricity generation process is an electricity generation process that converts chemical energy from a combustion fuel to electrical energy. It causes some emission."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [fuel-powered electricity generation process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050001) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "fuel-powered electricity generation"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [fuel-powered electricity generation process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050001) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/582
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/592

Update definition and axioms:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/936
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1240

Relabel and add old label as alternative term:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1455
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1562

replace energy-participant axioms
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/2053
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/2120"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [fuel-powered electricity generation process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050001) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "fuel-powered electricity generation process"@en

- [fuel-powered electricity generation process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050001) EquivalentTo [electricity gain process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240014) and ([has physical input](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000532) some [combustion fuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000099))

- [fuel-powered electricity generation process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050001) SubClassOf [has process attribute](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000500) some [emission factor](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000148)

### fueled power unit `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000175`

- [fueled power unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000175) EquivalentTo [power generating unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000334) and ([uses](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000503) some [fuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000173))

- [fueled power unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000175) SubClassOf [power generating unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000334)

### gaseous `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000182`

- [gaseous](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000182) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The state of being a gas. restructure modules:

pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1652

Fix definition:
https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1980"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [has author](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000506) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "The construct 'has author' some ('has role' some author) is necessary, because 'has author' has the range 'has role some author' (PR https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/871). That is an independent continuant, i.e. a person, that takes over writing a text and as such the role of an author."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### heat gain process `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010248`

#### Added
- Class: [heat gain process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010248)

- [heat gain process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010248) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A heat gain process is an energy transformation that has thermal energy as energy output."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [heat gain process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010248) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "heat generation process"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [heat gain process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010248) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "heat generation"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [heat gain process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010248) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1045
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1130

Add alternative term:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1455
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1562

replace energy-participant axioms
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/2053
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/2120

relabel
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/2053
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/2120"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [heat gain process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010248) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "With the logical definition of heat gain process, heat transfer is inferred as subclass which is intended but depends on the perspective and the system boundaries. To avoid confusion, the main label was changed from heat generation to heat gain process."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [heat gain process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010248) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "heat gain process"@en

- [heat gain process](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010248) SubClassOf [energy transformation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020003)

### is about `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136`

#### Added
- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "7/6/2009 Alan Ruttenberg. Following discussion with Jonathan Rees, and introduction of &quot;mentions&quot; relation. Weaken the is_about relationship to be primitive.

We will try to build it back up by elaborating the various subproperties that are more precisely defined.

Some currently missing phenomena that should be considered &quot;about&quot; are predications - &quot;The only person who knows the answer is sitting beside me&quot; , Allegory, Satire, and other literary forms that can be topical without explicitly mentioning the topic."@en

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) [isDefinedBy](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#isDefinedBy) [iao-extracted.owl](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/imports/iao-extracted.owl)

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) InverseOf [is subject of](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010231)

- [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136) SubPropertyOf: [topObjectProperty](http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#topObjectProperty)

### madde miktarı `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/AmountOfSubstance`

#### Added
- Individual: [madde miktarı](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/AmountOfSubstance)

- [madde miktarı](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/AmountOfSubstance) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "
  $\textit{Amount of Substance}$ is a standards-defined quantity that measures the size of an ensemble of elementary entities,
   such as atoms, molecules, electrons, and other particles.
  It is sometimes referred to as chemical amount.

  The International System of Units (SI) defines the amount of substance to be proportional to the number of elementary entities present.
  The SI unit for amount of substance is mole.
  It has the unit symbol $mol$.

  The mole is defined as the amount of substance that contains an equal number of elementary entities as there are atoms in 0.012kg of the isotope carbon-12.
  This number is called Avogadro's number and has the value $6.02214179(30) \times 10^{23}$.

  The only other unit of amount of substance in current use is the $pound-mole$ with the symbol $lb-mol$,
   which is sometimes used in chemical engineering in the United States.
  One $pound-mole$ is exactly $453.59237 mol$.
  "^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [madde miktarı](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/AmountOfSubstance) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A1E0L0I0M0H0T0D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A1E0L0I0M0H0T0D0)

- [madde miktarı](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/AmountOfSubstance) [symbol](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/symbol) "n"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### manufactured commodity `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010491`

#### Added
- Class: [manufactured commodity](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010491)

- [manufactured commodity](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010491) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A manufactured commodity is a portion of matter that is the physical output of a manufacturing process and that has a commodity role."@en

- [manufactured commodity](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010491) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "industrial commodity"@en

- [manufactured commodity](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010491) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "Split 'industrial material' into 'manufactured material' and 'manufactured commodity':
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1726
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1840"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [manufactured commodity](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010491) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "manufactured commodity"@en

- [manufactured commodity](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010491) SubClassOf [portion of matter](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000331)

- [manufactured material](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240026) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "industrial material"@en

### manufacturing process `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050000`

### mathematical model `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020354`

#### Added

### medium uncertainty `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400019`

#### Added
- Individual: [medium uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400019)

- [medium uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400019) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Medium uncertainty is a level of model uncertainty at which one can enumerate multiple alternatives and rank them in order of perceived likelihood, without being able to discern how intensely the likelihood varies between two alternatives."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [medium uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400019) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/264816512_Classifying_and_communicating_uncertainties_in_model-based_policy_analysis"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [medium uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400019) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1829
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1945"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [medium uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400019) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "medium uncertainty"@en

- [medium uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400019) Type [level of model uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400017)

### nace_ sectors `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000291`

#### Added
- Individual: [nace_ sectors](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000291)

- [nace_ sectors](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000291) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The Statistical classification of economic activities in the European Community, abbreviated as NACE, is the classification of economic activities in the European Union (EU); the term NACE is derived from the French Nomenclature statistique des activit&eacute;s &eacute;conomiques dans la Communaut&eacute; europ&eacute;enne. Various NACE versions have been developed since 1970.

NACE is a four-digit classification providing the framework for collecting and presenting a large range of statistical data according to economic activity in the fields of economic statistics (e.g. production, employment and national accounts) and in other statistical domains developed within the European statistical system (ESS)."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [nace_ sectors](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000291) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/statistics-explained/index.php?title=Glossary:Statistical_classification_of_economic_activities_in_the_European_Community_(NACE)"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [nace_ sectors](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000291) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "move to oeo-sector issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1724"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [nace_ sectors](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000291) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "nace_ sectors"@en

- [nace_ sectors](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000291) Type [sector division](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000368)


### nature of model uncertainty `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400013`

### net electricity generation `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240013`

#### Added
- Class: [net electricity generation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240013)

- [net electricity generation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240013) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Net electricity generation is a process attribute that equals to gross electricity generation minus the consumption of power stations' auxiliary services."@en

* Hard coal has a fossil origin and if it is used in a coal power plant to generate electrical energy, the electrical energy inherits the fossil origin.
* Wind energy has renewable origin and if it is converted to electrical energy using a wind turbine, the electrical energy inherits the renewable origin"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### physical unit `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010490`

#### Added
- Class: [physical unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010490)

- [physical unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010490) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A physical unit is a unit that represents a standardised quantity value of a physical quantifiable entity."@en

- [physical unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010490) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "The OEO developers have different views on the correct classification of a (physical) unit. On one hand, a (physical) unit is often referred to be &quot;a standardized quantity&quot;, e.g. in the UO, and thus is interpreted as a quantity itself. On the other hand, a (physical) unit can be seen as a generically dependent reference measure that is not bound to a specific bearer. Together with the numerical value it can describe a quantity value. In the OEO, we have opted for the latter view and therefore implemented (physical) unit as generically dependent continuants."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [physical unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010490) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "physikalische Einheit"@en

- [physical unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010490) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1871
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1892
remove equivalence axiom to uo:unit
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/2135
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/2141"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [physical unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010490) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "physical unit"@en

- [physical unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010490) SubClassOf [unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010489)

### portion of matter `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000331`

#### Added
- Class: [portion of matter](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000331)

- [portion of matter](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000331) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A portion of matter is an aggregate of material entities that have a state of matter."@en

- [portion of matter](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000331) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "Portions of matter describe mass nouns like e.g. water, air and fuel. Mass nouns are uncountable, but portions of these can be quantified, like 1 glass of water, 1 m&sup3; of air or 1 barrel of fuel. For further explanation please refer to: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/wiki/Explanation-on-mass-nouns"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [portion of matter](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000331) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/227
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/279

### prescribed by `https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001920`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [prescribed by](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001920)

- [prescribed by](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001920) [definition](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#definition) "x prescribed_by y iff y is an instance of Information Content Entity and x is an instance of Entity, such that y serves as a rule or guide for x if x is an Occurrent, or y serves as a model for x if x is a Continuant."@en

- [prescribed by](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001920) [ont00001760](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001760) "https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/InformationEntityOntology"^^[anyURI](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI)

- [prescribed by](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001920) [isDefinedBy](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#isDefinedBy) [cco-extracted.owl](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/imports/cco-extracted.owl)

- [prescribed by](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001920) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "prescribed by"@en

- [prescribed by](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001920) InverseOf [prescribes](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001942)

- [prescribed by](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001920) SubPropertyOf: [is subject of](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010231)


### prescribes `https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001942`

#### Added
- ObjectProperty: [prescribes](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001942)

- [prescribes](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001942) [definition](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#definition) "x prescribes y iff x is an instance of Information Content Entity and y is an instance of Entity, such that x serves as a rule or guide for y if y an Occurrent, or x serves as a model for y if y is a Continuant."@en

- [prescribes](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001942) [example](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#example) "A blueprint prescribes some artifact or facility by being a model for it."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [prescribes](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001942) [example](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#example) "A professional code of conduct prescribes some realizations of a profession (role) by giving rules for how the bearer should act in those realizations."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [prescribes](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001942) [example](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#example) "An operations plan prescribes an operation by enumerating the tasks that need to be performed in order to achieve the objectives of the operation."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [prescribes](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001942) [ont00001760](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001760) "https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/InformationEntityOntology"^^[anyURI](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI)

- [prescribes](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001942) [isDefinedBy](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#isDefinedBy) [cco-extracted.owl](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/imports/cco-extracted.owl)

- [prescribes](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001942) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "prescribes"@en

- [prescribes](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00001942) SubPropertyOf: [is about](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000136)

### primary energy consumption value `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050018`

#### Added
- Class: [primary energy consumption value](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050018)

- [primary energy consumption value](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050018) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A primary energy consumption value is an energy consumption value expressing the magnitude of the total consumption of energy in a spatial region excluding the non-energetic use of fuels."@en

- [primary energy consumption value](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050018) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "Primary energy consumption covers consumption of the energy sector itself, losses during transformation (for example, from oil or gas into electricity) and distribution of energy, and the final consumption by end users. It excludes energy carriers used for non-energy purposes (such as petroleum not used not for combustion but for producing plastics)."@en

- [primary energy consumption value](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050018) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Primary energy consumption excluding non-energy use of energy carriers."@en

- [primary energy consumption value](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050018) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Prim&auml;renergieverbrauch"@de

- [primary energy consumption value](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050018) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "Eurostat Energy Glossary
https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/statistics-explained/index.php?title=Glossary:Primary_energy_consumption"@en

- [primary energy consumption value](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050018) [OEO_00020425](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020425) "study descriptor tag"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [primary energy consumption value](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050018) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/709
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/719

issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/576
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/613

make energy consumption value:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1129
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1306

Move to oeo-shared:
https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1463
rework module structure
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1592
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1652

add oekg annotation
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/2063
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/2065"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [primary energy consumption value](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050018) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "primary energy consumption value"@en

- [primary energy consumption value](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00050018) SubClassOf [energy consumption value](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240019)

### quantity `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00340062`

#### Added

- [quantity](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00340062) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A quantity is a quality of a material entity where the magnitude  of the quality can be quantified via a quantity value, which means that it can be expressed as a number and a unit."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### rated voltage `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410068`

#### Added
- Class: [rated voltage](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410068)

- [rated voltage](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410068) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Rated voltage is a Performance Specification that prescribes the intended operating voltage of some electrical artifact."@en

- [rated voltage](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410068) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "maximum voltage"@en

- [rated voltage](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410068) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "definition adapted from: https://dlr-ve-esy.github.io/charging-ontology/CHIO_00000091.html"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [rated voltage](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410068) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "first implementation
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/2107
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/2108"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [rated voltage](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410068) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "Rated voltage usually means maximum voltage."@en

- [rated voltage](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410068) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "rated voltage"@en

- [rated voltage](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410068) SubClassOf [Performance Specification](https://www.commoncoreontologies.org/ont00000127)


### ratio `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0010006`

#### Added
- Class: [ratio](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0010006)

- [ratio](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0010006) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "&quot;A dimensionless ratio unit which, given a pair of quantities a and b, for which b is a multiple of a, denotes b by giving the multiplier (coefficient) c for a to result in b.&quot; [UOB:LKSR]"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [ratio](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0010006) [isDefinedBy](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#isDefinedBy) [uo.owl](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/uo.owl)

- [ratio](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0010006) SubClassOf [ratio unit](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000190)

### recognised ignorance `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400021`

- [recognised ignorance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400021) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Recognised ignorance is a level of model uncertainty at which one isn't able to enumerate multiple alternatives, while admitting the possibility of being surprised."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1945"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [recognised ignorance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400021) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "recognised ignorance"@en

- [recognised ignorance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400021) Type [level of model uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400017)


### redox reaction `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140034`

### regulatory instrument `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010458`

- Class: [regulatory instrument](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010458)

- [regulatory instrument](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010458) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A regulatory instrument is a policy instrument that stipulates rules made by a government (=institution) to control the way something is done."@en

- [regulatory instrument](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010458) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1545
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1786

Convert to equivalent class:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1826
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1906"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [regulatory instrument](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010458) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "regulatory instrument"@en

- [regulatory instrument](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010458) SubClassOf [policy instrument](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140151)

### report `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000088`

#### Added
- [report](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000088) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "2009-03-16: comment from Darren Natale: I am slightly uneasy with the sentence &quot;Topic of the report is on
something that has completed.&quot;  Should it be restricted to those things
that are completed?  For example, a progress report is (usually) about
something that definitely has *not* been completed, or may include
(only) projections.  I think the definition would not suffer if the
whole sentence is deleted."@en

- [report](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000088) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "Add 'has licence' axiom:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1275
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1547

rework module structure and move axiom to oeo-shared-axioms
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1592
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1652"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [report](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000088) [isDefinedBy](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#isDefinedBy) [iao-extracted.owl](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/imports/iao-extracted.owl)

- [report](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000088) SubClassOf [has licence](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020188) some [creative work licence](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010412)

### service product role `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00360018`

- Class: [service product role](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00360018)

- [service product role](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00360018) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A service product role is the role of an immaterial entity that is the result of a service process, and whose value is determined by the value and the costs of the service."@en

### shallow uncertainty `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400018`

#### Added
- Individual: [shallow uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400018)

- [shallow uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400018) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Shallow uncertainty is a level of model uncertainty at which one can enumerate multiple alternatives and provide probabilites(subjective or objective) for them."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [shallow uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400018) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "https://www.researchgate.net/publication/264816512_Classifying_and_communicating_uncertainties_in_model-based_policy_analysis"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1945"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [shallow uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400018) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "shallow uncertainty"@en

- [shallow uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400018) Type [level of model uncertainty](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00400017)

### solid `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000390`

#### Added
- Individual: [solid](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000390)

- [solid](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000390) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The state of being solid. A portion of matter is in solid state if and only if it has a definite shape and volume (at a given temperature and pressure)."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/2019"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [solid](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000390) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "solid"@en

- [solid](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000390) Type [state of matter](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000395)


### solid biofuel `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000332`

#### Added
- Class: [solid biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000332)

- [solid biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000332) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Solid biomass is a portion of matter consisting of organic, non-fossil material of biological origin which may be used as fuel for heat production or electricity generation."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [solid biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000332) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "biogener Festbrennstoff"@de

- [solid biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000332) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "portion of solid biomass"@en

- [solid biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000332) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "solid biomass"@en

- [solid biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000332) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:32008R1099&amp;from=EN"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [solid biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000332) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "solid biofuel"@en

- [solid biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000332) EquivalentTo [biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000072) and ([has normal state of matter](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000529) value [solid](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000390))

- [solid biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000332) SubClassOf [has normal state of matter](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000529) value [solid](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000390)

- [solid biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000332) SubClassOf [has origin](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000530) some [biogenic](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00030001)

- [solid biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000332) SubClassOf [biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000072)
  - [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "Add `SubClass Of` axiom for `solid biofuel`

- [spoken by](https://w3id.org/energy-intel/spokenBy) Range [Expert](https://w3id.org/energy-intel/Expert)

- [spoken by](https://w3id.org/energy-intel/spokenBy) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "Binary participation shortcut for podcast speakers (multi-speaker segments allowed; no cardinality axiom). V1: range widened to foaf:Person; SHACL S-V1-4 carries the EnergyExpertRole-bearer warning at runtime."@en

### state of matter `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000395`

#### Added
- Class: [state of matter](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000395)

- [state of matter](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000395) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A state of matter is a quality of a portion of matter that describes the distinct physical form in which the matter exists."@en

- [state of matter](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000395) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "Four states of matter are observable in everyday life: solid, liquid, gas, and plasma. Many intermediate states are known to exist, such as liquid crystal, and some states only exist under extreme conditions."@en

### steel `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240034`

#### Added
- Class: [steel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240034)

- [steel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00240034) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Steel is a metal that consists mainly of iron and up to 2.14 % of carbon; other elements may be present. It has a solid normal state of matter. It has a solid normal state of matter. It is the physical output of an industrial process and has a commodity role."@en

### study `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020011`

#### Added
- Class: [study](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020011)

- [study](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020011) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A study is a project with the goal to methodologically investigate something."@en

- [study](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020011) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "The construct 'has funding source' some ('has role' some funder) is necessary, because has funding source has the range has role some funder. This range comes from issue https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/850 : funding source is some kind of independent continuant, i.e. a organisation or person, that does something special (giving money). So it is anything that has role some funder."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [study](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020011) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Studie"@de

### study region `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020032`

#### Added
- Class: [study region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020032)

- [study region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020032) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "In a study, the spatial focus of the investigation lies on one European country, e.g. Germany. This is the study region.
The study region is not modelled as a whole, but several subregions are modelled, e.g. German Federal States. These are study subregions.
In the study, a spatial region outside the study region plays a role. E.g. for modelling the electricity transfer between Germany and its neighbouring countries. In this example, the neighbouring countries represent the interacting / external region.
The union of the study region and the interacting/ external region is the considered region."@en

- [study region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020032) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A study region is a region of relevance that has the study region role and consists entirely of one or more subregions."@en

- [study region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020032) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/475
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/585

rework module structure
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1592
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1652

relocated, made equivalent
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1745
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1749"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [study region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020032) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "study region"@en

- [study region](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020032) SubClassOf [region of relevance](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00360020)


### study region role `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00360021`

#### Added
- Class: [study region role](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00360021)

- [study region role](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00360021) [example of usage](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000112) "In a study, the spatial focus of the investigation lies on one European country, e.g. Germany. This is the study region.
The study region is not modelled as a whole, but several subregions are modelled, e.g. German Federal States. These are study subregions.
In the study, a spatial region outside the study region plays a role. E.g. for modelling the electricity transfer between Germany and its neighbouring countries. In this example, the neighbouring countries represent the interacting / external region.
The union of the study region and the interacting/ external region ist the considered region."@en

- [study region role](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00360021) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A study region role is a role of a region of relevance that is under investigation and consists entirely of one or more subregions."@en

### sustainability criterion `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010332`

#### Added
- Class: [sustainability criterion](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010332)

- [sustainability criterion](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010332) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A sustainability criterion is a plan specification that intends to meet the needs of the present without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their own environmental, economic and social needs."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [sustainability criterion](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010332) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Nachhaltigkeitskriterium"@de

- [sustainability criterion](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010332) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/872
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1385"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [sustainability criterion](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010332) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "sustainability criterion"@en

- [sustainability criterion](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010332) SubClassOf [plan specification](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000104)


### sustainable biofuel `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010336`

- [sustainable biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010336) SubClassOf [biofuel](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000072)

### typical period `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020089`

#### Added
- Class: [typical period](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020089)

- [typical period](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020089) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A typical period is a time series that represents a couple of real time periods that have certain characteristics in common, e.g. seasonal specifications, weekly routines, etc. It doesn't refer to a real date. Typical periods are used to reduce computing time of energy system models."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [typical period](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020089) [definition source](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000119) "Definition adapted from: https://www.enargus.de/pub/bscw.cgi/d13068-2/*/*/Typtag.html?op=Wiki.getwiki&amp;scope=all"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [typical period](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020089) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "add axiom `uses some 'aggregation type'`:
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/880
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/883

issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/830
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/875"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [typical period](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020089) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "typical period"@en

- [typical period](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020089) SubClassOf [time series](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00030034)

- [typical period](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020089) SubClassOf [uses](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00000503) some [aggregation type](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140068)


### uncertainty approach `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140167`

#### Added
- Class: [uncertainty approach](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140167)

- [uncertainty approach](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140167) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "An uncertainty approach is a model descriptor that specifies how a model deals with uncertainty."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [uncertainty approach](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140167) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/183
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/824"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [uncertainty approach](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140167) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "uncertainty approach"@en

- [uncertainty approach](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00140167) SubClassOf [model descriptor](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020016)


### unit `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010489`

#### Added
- Class: [unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010489)

- [unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010489) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A unit of measurement is a generically dependent continuant that represents a standardised quantity value of a quantifiable entity"@en

- [unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010489) [editor note](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000116) "The OEO developers have different views on the correct classification of a (physical) unit. On one hand, a (physical) unit is often referred to be &quot;a standardized quantity&quot;, e.g. in the UO, and thus is interpreted as a quantity itself. On the other hand, a (physical) unit can be seen as a generically dependent reference measure that is not bound to a specific bearer. Together with the numerical value it can describe a quantity value. In the OEO, we have opted for the latter view and therefore implemented (physical) unit as generically dependent continuants."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010489) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Einheit"@de

- [unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010489) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "Ma&szlig;einheit"@de

- [unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010489) [alternative label](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000118) "unit of measurement"@en

- [unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010489) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1871
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/1892"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [unit](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00010489) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "unit"@en

### vatio `http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/W`

#### Added
- Individual: [vatio](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/W)

- [vatio](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/W) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "The SI unit of power. Power is the rate at which work is done, or (equivalently) the rate at which energy is expended. One watt is equal to a power rate of one joule of work per second of time. This unit is used both in mechanics and in electricity, so it links the mechanical and electrical units to one another. In mechanical terms, one watt equals about 0.001 341 02 horsepower (hp) or 0.737 562 foot-pound per second (lbf/s). In electrical terms, one watt is the power produced by a current of one ampere flowing through an electric potential of one volt. The name of the unit honors James Watt (1736-1819), the British engineer whose improvements to the steam engine are often credited with igniting the Industrial Revolution."^^[HTML](http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#HTML)

- [vatio](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/W) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-EMU](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-EMU)

- [vatio](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/W) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-GAUSS](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-GAUSS)

- [vatio](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/W) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS)

- [vatio](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/W) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [PLANCK](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/PLANCK)

- [vatio](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/W) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [SI](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/SI)

### volt `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000218`

#### Added
- Class: [volt](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000218)

- [volt](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000218) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "&quot;An electric potential difference unit which is equal to the work per unit charge. One volt is the potential difference required to move one coulomb of charge between two points in a circuit while using one joule of energy.&quot; [Wikipedia:Wikipedia]"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [volt](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000218) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "V"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [volt](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000218) [isDefinedBy](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#isDefinedBy) [uo.owl](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/uo.owl)

- [volt](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000218) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "volt"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [volt](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_0000218) SubClassOf [volt based unit](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UO_1000218)


### volt `http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/V`

#### Added
- Individual: [volt](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/V)

- [volt](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/V) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "$\textit{Volt}$ is the SI unit of electric potential.
  Separating electric charges creates potential energy, which can be measured in energy units such as joules.
  Electric potential is defined as the amount of potential energy present per unit of charge.
  Electric potential is measured in volts, with one volt representing a potential of one joule per coulomb of charge.
  The name of the unit honors the Italian scientist Count Alessandro Volta (1745-1827), the inventor of the first battery.
  The volt also may be expressed with a variety of other units.
  For example, a volt is also equal to one watt per ampere ($W/A$) and one joule per ampere per second ($J/A/s$).
  "^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [volt](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/V) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-EMU](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-EMU)

- [volt](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/V) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-GAUSS](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-GAUSS)

- [volt](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/V) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [PLANCK](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/PLANCK)

- [volt](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/V) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [SI](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/SI)

- [volt](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/V) [conversionMultiplier](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/conversionMultiplier) 1.0

- [volt](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/V) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E-1L2I0M1H0T-3D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E-1L2I0M1H0T-3D0)

### voltage classification system `https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410058`

#### Added
- Class: [voltage classification system](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410058)

- [voltage classification system](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410058) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A voltage classification system is an information content entity that categorizes the voltages that components of an electricity grid power system usually operate on, into different levels."@en

- [voltage classification system](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410058) [term tracker annotation](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00020426) "issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1960
pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/2042"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [voltage classification system](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410058) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "Voltage classifications are crucial because they determine system requirements, equipment standards, and safety protocols. These classifications serve as a framework that industries&mdash;ranging from utility networks to manufacturers&mdash;use to design systems and equipment in accordance with predetermined voltage levels. By standardizing voltage levels, it ensures that the systems operate safely, efficiently, and consistently across different applications and regions. (source: https://missioncriticalengineers.com/voltage-classifications/ accessed 2025-07-04)"@en

- [voltage classification system](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410058) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "voltage classification system"@en

- [voltage classification system](https://openenergyplatform.org/ontology/oeo/OEO_00410058) SubClassOf [information content entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000030)

pull request: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/pull/484

move to oeo-sector
issue: https://github.com/OpenEnergyPlatform/ontology/issues/1399

### χερτζ `http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/HZ`

#### Added
- Individual: [χερτζ](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/HZ)

- [χερτζ](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/HZ) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "
  $\textit{Hertz}$ (symbol $Hz$) is the SI unit of frequency defined as the number of cycles per second of a periodic phenomenon.
  One of its most common uses is the description of the sine wave, particularly those used in radio and audio applications, such as the frequency of musical tones.
  The word $\bf{hertz}$ is named for Heinrich Rudolf Hertz, who was the first to conclusively prove the existence of electromagnetic waves.
  "^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [χερτζ](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/HZ) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-EMU](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-EMU)

### килограмм `http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloGM`

#### Added
- Individual: [килограмм](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloGM)

- [килограмм](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloGM) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "The kilogram or kilogramme (SI symbol: kg), also known as the kilo, is the base unit of mass in the International System of Units and is defined as being equal to the mass of the International Prototype Kilogram (IPK), which is almost exactly equal to the mass of one liter of water. The avoirdupois (or international) pound, used in both the Imperial system and U.S. customary units, is defined as exactly 0.45359237 kg, making one kilogram approximately equal to 2.2046 avoirdupois pounds."^^[HTML](http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#HTML)

- [килограмм](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloGM) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-EMU](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-EMU)

- [килограмм](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloGM) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-GAUSS](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-GAUSS)

- [килограмм](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloGM) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS)

- [килограмм](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloGM) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [SI](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/SI)

- [килограмм](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloGM) [conversionMultiplier](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/conversionMultiplier) 1.0

- [килограмм](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloGM) [hasDimensionVector](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/hasDimensionVector) [A0E0L0I0M1H0T0D0](http://qudt.org/vocab/dimensionvector/A0E0L0I0M1H0T0D0)

- [килограмм](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloGM) [plainTextDescription](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/plainTextDescription) "The kilogram or kilogramme (SI symbol: kg), also known as the kilo, is the base unit of mass in the International System of Units and is defined as being equal to the mass of the International Prototype Kilogram (IPK), which is almost exactly equal to the mass of one liter of water. The avoirdupois (or international) pound, used in both the Imperial system and U.S. customary units, is defined as exactly 0.45359237 kg, making one kilogram approximately equal to 2.2046 avoirdupois pounds."^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

- [килограмм](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/KiloGM) [symbol](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/symbol) "kg"^^[string](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string)

### אמפר `http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/A`

#### Added
- Individual: [אמפר](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/A)

- [אמפר](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/A) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "$\textit{Ampere}$, often shortened to $\text{amp}$,
is the SI unit of electric current and is one of the seven SI base units defined as:

$$\text{A} \equiv \frac{\textit{C}}{\textit{s}}
\equiv \frac{\textit{coulomb}}{\textit{second}}
\equiv \frac{\text{joule}}{\text{weber}}$$

Note that SI supports only the use of symbols and deprecates the use of any abbreviations for units.
"^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)

- [אמפר](http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/A) [applicableSystem](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/applicableSystem) [CGS-EMU](http://qudt.org/vocab/sou/CGS-EMU)

### جرم `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Mass`

### शक्ति `http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Power`

#### Added
- Individual: [शक्ति](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Power)

- [शक्ति](http://qudt.org/vocab/quantitykind/Power) [Description](http://purl.org/dc/terms/description) "Power is the rate at which work is performed or energy is transmitted, or the amount of energy required or expended for a given unit of time. As a rate of change of work done or the energy of a subsystem, power is: $P = W/t$, where $P$ is power, $W$ is work and {t} is time."^^[LatexString](http://qudt.org/schema/qudt/LatexString)
